sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM

symptom: API calls return HTTP 429. Error 429 visible in HttpOutgoingRequests table matching client-side throttling reports
rootCause: Resource Provider is throttling requests (not ARM). RP returning 429 responses to ARM's outgoing requests.
solution: Transfer case to the RP-owning team. RP throttling limits may sometimes be adjustable; all recommendations on avoiding RP throttle limits must come from the team supporting that RP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM

symptom: HTTP 429 throttling on specific ARM resource type operations (e.g., Microsoft.Insights/scheduledqueryrules). Response headers include x-ms-ratelimit-remaining-subscription-resource-requests.
rootCause: Some Resource Providers define custom throttlingRules in their ARM manifests that override default ARM limits (e.g., Microsoft.Insights limits scheduledqueryrules/write to 60 requests/hour). This results in ARM-layer failure caused by RP override.
solution: Check the RP manifest at armmanifest.visualstudio.com for throttlingRules for the affected resource type. Apply standard ARM throttling mitigations: distribute calls across service principals, reduce call frequency. The x-ms-ratelimit-remaining-subscription-resource-requests header only appears when the default ARM limit is overridden.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Support Topics/Throttling/Throttling (Error 429) response from ARM

symptom: Resource exists in Resource Provider but doesn't appear in ARM; or resource appears in ARM but no longer exists in RP (ARM cache desync)
rootCause: ARM cache is out of sync with the actual Resource Provider state for a specific resource at a specific location
solution: Use Jarvis 'Synchronize resource state' action targeting the specific resource and location. Select provisioning state 'Create' if resource exists in RP but not ARM; select 'Delete' if resource exists in ARM but not in RP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureDev/Dev_ARM:/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Troubleshoting Guides/ARM cache synchronization
sourceUrl: https://dev.azure.com/Supportability/AzureDev/_wiki/wikis/Dev_ARM?pagePath=/[ARM] Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Troubleshoting Guides/ARM cache synchronization

symptom: Hyper-V components (Hyper-V management tools) not installing on a random HCI node during PP2 integrated ASDK deployment, causing the HCI environment validator script (3-InfraDeploy.ps1) to fail
rootCause: Intermittent issue where Hyper-V tools get skipped on a random HCI node during the automated deployment script execution. Root cause not fully determined (extra logging added to investigate).
solution: Reinstall Hyper-V management tools on affected node via PowerShell: `$clusterAdminCred = New-Object -Type System.Management.Automation.PSCredential "winfield\$($ClDomainUser)", $NodeCredential.Password; $x = (1..$HciNodeCount); foreach($y in $x) { Invoke-Command -VMName "HCI-N$($y)" -Credential $clusterAdminCred -ScriptBlock { Install-WindowsFeature -Name Hyper-V -IncludeManagementTools -Verbose }}`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Beta Resources - Pre-GA/Deployment/PP2 Integrated ASDK Install

symptom: PP2 integrated ASDK deployment scripts fail or behave unexpectedly when Azure PowerShell (Az PS) module version is newer than 8.2.0
rootCause: Specific Az PowerShell module versions beyond 8.2.0 introduce breaking changes that conflict with the deployment scripts. Exact breaking version not documented.
solution: Use Azure PowerShell module version 8.2.0 or earlier. Do not update beyond 8.2.0 until a compatible version is identified.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Beta Resources - Pre-GA/Deployment/PP2 Integrated ASDK Install

symptom: ARC-A external service endpoints (e.g. *.blob.autonomous.cloud.private) unreachable from clients outside the VM host
rootCause: Wildcard DNS record not configured in host environment DNS server; external DNS names do not resolve to the ingress NIC IP of the Arc-A VM
solution: Configure a wildcard DNS record in the host environment DNS server resolving the ARC-A domain (e.g. *.autonomous.cloud.private) to the ingress NIC IP address of the Arc-A VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Components/Layer 1 - Host Environment/Network

symptom: Arc-A VM is not highly available or experiences data loss across reboots
rootCause: Arc-A VM disks (OS, Ephemeral, Local) not placed on HA storage from the VM host's perspective, leaving them vulnerable to single points of failure
solution: Ensure all three Arc-A VM virtual hard disks (OS disk, Ephemeral disk, Local persistent data disk) are provisioned on HA storage provided by the VM host environment
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Components/Layer 1 - Host Environment/Storage

symptom: Customer needs to configure Azure Local Disconnected Operations (ALDO) VM appliance after downloading and powering it on — IP settings, DNS forwarder, and Azure subscription for Arc connection and log upload
solution: Use the System Configuration Service (SCS), which is bound to the management vNIC. SCS handles configuration of: (1) IP settings of the ingress NIC, (2) DNS forwarder, (3) Azure subscription for connecting the machine to Arc and log upload. Access SCS through the management NIC API endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Components/Layer 2 - Runtime/Local Services/Agent Lifecycle Mgmt/System Configuration Service (SCS)

symptom: Azure Local disconnected operations deployment fails with 2-node HCI cluster due to cluster quorum requirements
rootCause: Disconnected HCI cannot use Azure blob storage as cloud witness for cluster quorum (no cloud connectivity). 2-node HCI clusters require a cloud witness, which is unavailable in disconnected mode.
solution: Use a 3-node HCI cluster for disconnected operations deployment. A 3-node cluster does not require an external witness for quorum. Future support for local file-share witness (enabling 2-node deployment) has been requested to the HCI team but is not yet available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] Deployment Requirements

symptom: Azure Local disconnected operations infrastructure VM (Winfield) does not start after deployment
rootCause: Insufficient resources allocated to the VM or incorrect network configuration (virtual switches not set up correctly)
solution: 1. Verify VM has recommended resources: 24 vCPUs, 48 GB RAM. 2. Check network configuration: ensure internal virtual switches (Winfield-Ingress and Winfield-Management) are correctly set up. 3. Verify NAT rule is correctly configured to allow traffic to the appliance VM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] Initial deployment of Winfield VM and portal startup

symptom: Azure Local disconnected operations portal (Project Winfield) is not accessible after infrastructure VM starts
rootCause: Incorrect IP address configuration or self-signed certificate not installed on the host's trusted root certificate store
solution: 1. Verify IP addresses: management endpoint (169.254.53.25) and portal endpoint (10.0.50.4) must be correctly configured. 2. Install self-signed certificate from management endpoint on host's trusted root certificate store. 3. Modify host file to add DNS records resolving Project Winfield appliance endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] Initial deployment of Winfield VM and portal startup

symptom: Network connectivity issues between host and Azure Local disconnected operations appliance VM
rootCause: Incorrect network adapter settings or DNS server not configured correctly on the appliance VM
solution: 1. Verify network settings on appliance VM: ensure two network adapters and their respective IP addresses are correctly configured. 2. Ensure DNS server IP is correctly set to resolve external endpoints. 3. Use Get-WinfieldApplianceSettings cmdlet to verify the applied settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] Initial deployment of Winfield VM and portal startup

symptom: Unable to verify connection to Azure Local disconnected operations Project Winfield endpoints
rootCause: Incorrect endpoint configuration or underlying network issues preventing connectivity to portal.autonomous.cloud.private
solution: 1. Run Invoke-WebRequest https://portal.autonomous.cloud.private/api/ping to test connectivity. 2. Check network settings and verify correct IP addresses are used. 3. Verify management endpoint is running using Get-WinfieldApplianceSettings cmdlet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] Initial deployment of Winfield VM and portal startup

symptom: Unable to verify connection to Azure Local disconnected operations Project Winfield endpoints after deployment
rootCause: Incorrect endpoint configuration or network issues preventing access to portal.autonomous.cloud.private
solution: 1. Run Invoke-WebRequest https://portal.autonomous.cloud.private/api/ping to verify endpoint connectivity. 2. Check network settings and ensure correct IP addresses are used. 3. Verify management endpoint is running via Get-WinfieldApplianceSettings cmdlet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] Initial deployment of Winfield VM and portal startup

symptom: Azure Local (HCI) VM Extension 'AzureEdgeDeviceManagement' fails to enable on cluster nodes with error: 'Failed to enable the Device Management Extension probably because the Resource Provider (Microsoft.AzureStackHCI) is not yet registered in the subscription'
rootCause: The Microsoft.AzureStackHCI resource provider is not registered in the subscription, or the extension state is corrupted. In Winfield/disconnected environments, the portal may not support re-installation.
solution: Uninstall and reinstall the extension via Azure CLI: 1) az cloud set -n azure.local && az login --use-device-code; 2) az connectedmachine extension delete --extension-name AzureEdgeDeviceManagement --machine-name <node> --resource-group <rg> --yes; 3) az connectedmachine extension create with original properties (publisher: Microsoft.Edge, type: DeviceManagementExtension, type-handler-version from step show).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] VM Extension failed in HCI cluster nodes

symptom: Azure Local deployment fails during 'Azure Stack HCI Connectivity' validation with error: 'DNS resolution failed on <node IP>. Queried dns server <DNS IP> for microsoft.com on <node>. Result returned 0 A records. Expected at least 1.'
rootCause: DNS forwarding is not configured on the DNS server. The local DC/DNS cannot resolve external domain names (e.g., microsoft.com) because no forwarders are set.
solution: Configure DNS forwarding on the DNS server (DC): Open DNS Manager -> right-click server -> Properties -> Forwarders tab -> add external DNS forwarders (e.g., 8.8.8.8 or ISP DNS). This allows the local DNS to resolve external names required for Azure connectivity checks.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Deployment failed with "Azure Stack HCI Connectivity"

symptom: Azure Local deployment fails during 'Download content for deployment' step with error: 'Fail to initialize cloud deployment: Either the target name is incorrect or the server has rejected the client credentials'
rootCause: An incorrect deployment username was provided during deployment setup. The username does not match the pre-created deployment user in the Active Directory OU.
solution: Update the deployment username to match the pre-created AD user. Use New-HciAdObjectsPreCreation to pre-create: $credential = New-Object PSCredential('<deployuser>', (ConvertTo-SecureString '<pwd>' -AsPlainText -Force)); New-HciAdObjectsPreCreation -AzureStackLCMUserCredential $credential -AsHciOUName 'OU=<HCIOU>,DC=<domain>,DC=local'. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-local/deploy/deployment-prep-active-directory
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Download content for deployment

symptom: Azure Local deployment fails during 'Join servers to a domain' with error: Type 'AddAsZHostToDomain' of Role 'BareMetal' raised an exception: Computer '<node>' failed to join domain '<domain>' - The specified domain either does not exist or could not be contacted.
rootCause: The primary DNS server configured on the node cannot resolve the AD domain. A non-DC DNS IP is listed first, and the system does not fallback to secondary DNS for domain resolution.
solution: Set the DC/DNS IP address as the first (primary) DNS entry on all cluster nodes. For example, if DC IP is 192.168.2.2, ensure it is the first DNS server in the NIC configuration. Then resume the deployment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Type 'AddAsZHostToDomain' of Role 'BareMetal' raised an exception

symptom: Azure Local deployment fails during 'Azure Stack HCI Network' validation: Type 'ValidateNetwork' of Role 'EnvironmentValidator' raised an exception. Storage adapter ping mesh test fails between nodes (APIPA addresses on Storage1/Storage2 cannot ping across nodes).
rootCause: Storage VLANs (e.g., 711, 712) are not properly configured on TOR switch ports. Switch ports may be in Access mode instead of Trunk mode, or VLAN IDs are not in the allowed list on both ports.
solution: 1) On TOR switches: configure storage switch ports as Trunk mode with VLANs in allowed list on BOTH ports. 2) For lab: Set-VMNetworkAdapterVlan -VMName <node> -Trunk -AllowedVlanIdList '711,712' -NativeVlanId 0. 3) Re-run: Invoke-AzStackHciNetworkValidation -DeployAnswerFile <path> -PSSession $sessions -ProxyEnabled $false. 4) Resume deployment. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-local/manage/use-environment-checker
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Type 'ValidateNetwork' of Role 'EnvironmentValidator' raised an exception

symptom: Azure Local (HCI) cluster node 上 VM Extension (AzureEdgeDeviceManagement) 安装失败，报错 'Failed to enable the Device Management Extension probably because the Resource Provider (Microsoft.AzureStackHCI) is not yet registered in the subscription'
rootCause: Microsoft.AzureStackHCI Resource Provider 未在订阅中注册，或扩展需要重新安装
solution: 1) 在 Azure Local cloud 上用 az CLI 登录 (az cloud set -n azure.local && az login --use-device-code); 2) az connectedmachine extension show 获取属性; 3) az connectedmachine extension delete 删除扩展; 4) az connectedmachine extension create 重新安装，publisher='Microsoft.Edge', type='DeviceManagementExtension'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Deployment Issues/[TSG] VM Extension failed in HCI cluster nodes

symptom: Azure Local 部署失败，报 'Azure Stack HCI Connectivity' 错误: DNS resolution failed，Queried dns server for microsoft.com 返回 0 A records
rootCause: DNS 服务器未配置 DNS 转发 (DNS Forwarder)，导致无法解析外部域名
solution: 在 DC/DNS 服务器上配置 DNS 转发 (DNS Forwarder)，添加正确的上游 DNS 服务器地址，使 DNS 服务器能转发无法本地解析的查询
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Deployment failed with "Azure Stack HCI Connectivity"

symptom: Azure Local 部署失败，步骤 'Download content for deployment' 报错 'Either the target name is incorrect or the server has rejected the client credentials'
rootCause: 部署时提供了错误的 deployment username（如用户名与 AD 中预创建的用户不匹配）
solution: 更新 deployment username 为正确值（通过 New-HciAdObjectsPreCreation 预创建的部署用户）。示例命令: New-HciAdObjectsPreCreation -AzureStackLCMUserCredential $credential -AsHciOUName 'OU=...,DC=...,DC=local'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Download content for deployment

symptom: Azure Local 部署失败，'Join servers to a domain' 步骤报错 'Type AddAsZHostToDomain of Role BareMetal raised an exception: Computer failed to join domain - The specified domain either does not exist or could not be contacted'
rootCause: 主 DNS 服务器 IP 配置错误，指向了非 DC/DNS 的 IP 地址（如外部 DNS），导致无法解析 AD 域名。且系统在首选 DNS 失败后不会尝试备用 DNS
solution: 将 DC/DNS 服务器 IP 设置为第一个 DNS 条目（首选 DNS），然后 Resume 部署即可修复
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Type 'AddAsZHostToDomain' of Role 'BareMetal' raised an exception

symptom: Azure Local 部署失败，'Azure Stack HCI Network' 验证报错 'Type ValidateNetwork of Role EnvironmentValidator raised an exception'，storage adapter connections ping mesh 在节点间全部 FAILED（Storage1/Storage2 适配器使用 169.254.x.x APIPA 地址）
rootCause: 存储网络适配器之间无法通信，VLAN 配置问题导致 Storage1/Storage2 适配器 ping mesh 失败。适配器显示 APIPA 地址 (169.254.x.x) 表明未正确获得 VLAN 内的 IP
solution: 1) 检查 C:\MASLogs 下的 answer file 获取网络配置; 2) 运行 Invoke-AzStackHciNetworkValidation -DeployAnswerFile $answerFilePath -PSSession $allServerSessions 进行网络验证; 3) 检查存储适配器 VLAN ID 配置是否正确（如 Storage1=711, Storage2=712）; 4) 确保物理网络交换机端口正确配置了对应 VLAN
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/Azure Local deployment networking issue/Type 'ValidateNetwork' of Role 'EnvironmentValidator' raised an exception

symptom: Azure Local/HCI cluster RDMA performance issues - Flow Control is enabled on physical NICs, causing global pause frames that impact all traffic flows instead of using PFC for selective pausing
rootCause: Flow Control (global pause) is enabled by default on physical NICs. When enabled, it sends global pause frames onto the network which pause ALL traffic types, including cluster heartbeat traffic, instead of only pausing the specific traffic class that needs throttling.
solution: Disable Flow Control on all physical NICs used for RDMA: Set-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty -Name <NIC> -RegistryKeyword '*FlowControl' -RegistryValue 0. Verify with Get-NetAdapterAdvancedProperty. PFC (Priority Flow Control) should be used instead, enabled only on the SMB priority level.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/RDMA Configuration

symptom: RDMA PFC (Priority Flow Control) not working on Azure Local cluster - switches treat RDMA/SMB traffic as normal network traffic despite QoS policies being configured
rootCause: VLAN tags are not configured on the storage network adapters. PFC information is contained within the VLAN header, so without VLAN tags the switches cannot see PFC information and treat all traffic as best-effort.
solution: Add VLAN tags to storage NICs. For non-converged setup: set VLAN ID in Advanced Properties of the pNIC. For converged setup: set VLAN IDs on vNICs only using Set-VMNetworkAdapterVlan -ManagementOS -VMNetworkAdapterName <name> -Access -VlanId <id>. Verify with Get-VMNetworkAdapterVlan -ManagementOS or Get-VMNetworkAdapterIsolation -ManagementOS. Do NOT set VLANs in both locations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/RDMA Configuration

symptom: Azure Local DCB/QoS settings unexpectedly changed or overwritten - RDMA configuration drift detected on HCI cluster nodes
rootCause: DCBX Willing mode is set to True (default), allowing DCB settings on the host to be overwritten by settings advertised by another source (e.g., network switches) participating in DCB exchange.
solution: Set DCBX Willing mode to False globally: Set-NetQosDcbxSetting -Willing $false. Verify with Get-NetQosDcbxSetting (global) and per-interface settings. If global is False, DCBX is disabled everywhere. If global is True, check per-interface settings. In ALDO deployments, Network ATC should handle this automatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/RDMA Configuration

symptom: RDMA Completion Queue Errors counter is non-zero on Azure Local/HCI nodes, indicating errors in RDMA Send or Receive queue operations
rootCause: Driver or firmware issue on the RDMA NIC causing errors in completion queue processing for RDMA operations.
solution: Update NIC driver and firmware to the latest versions. Check RDMA Completion Queue Errors via PerfMon counters. Also verify NetworkDirect Technology registry value (*NetworkDirectTechnology) is present - some vendors didn't include it in driver INF file; updating driver/firmware resolves this. Use Validate-DCB module (Install-Module Validate-DCB) to comprehensively validate RDMA/DCB configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Deployment and Integration/Networking/RDMA Configuration

symptom: Cannot collect diagnostic logs from Azure Local disconnected operations - standard log collection fails because the Azure Local VM (IRVM01) is down or disconnected
rootCause: The Azure Local disconnected operations VM (IRVM01) is offline, disconnected, or failed, preventing standard log collection from being initiated through normal channels.
solution: Use appliance fallback logging: 1) Import-Module ApplianceFallbackLogging.psm1, 2) Copy-DiagnosticData -DiagnosticLogPath <path> to mount VHDs and copy logs (requires BitLocker recovery keys), 3) Send-DiagnosticData with Azure credentials to upload logs to Microsoft. The IRVM is automatically restarted after Copy-DiagnosticData completes. Applies to Azure Local 2411.1+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Diagnostics and Monitoring/Fallback Log Collection

symptom: Customer can export Azure Local disconnected diagnostic logs with Copy-WinfieldDiagnosticData but cannot upload them with Send-WinfieldDiagnosticData
rootCause: The customer's environment cannot reach Azure for log upload, or the standalone observability pipeline fails to Arc-enable the host for upload. The customer has logs exported locally but no path to send them to Microsoft.
solution: Customer provides exported logs to CSS support engineer via approved DTM File Transfer process. Support engineer then runs Send-WinfieldDiagnosticData on behalf of the customer to upload logs to Kusto/Geneva workspace. Important: Support engineers should NOT run this on their primary work device as it Arc-enables the host machine.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Diagnostics and Monitoring/Fallback Logging

symptom: Unable to resolve domain name portal.autonomous.cloud.private when accessing Project Winfield portal in Azure Local disconnected operations
rootCause: DNS name resolution not configured for Winfield private endpoints on client machine
solution: Add host entries to the client hosts file (Windows: C:\Windows\system32\drivers\etc\hosts, Linux: /etc/hosts) mapping the Winfield appliance IP (e.g. 10.0.50.4) to portal.autonomous.cloud.private, login.autonomous.cloud.private, and graph.autonomous.cloud.private. Verify with: ping portal.autonomous.cloud.private
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Unable to install the self-signed certificate (Winfield.cer) on the client computer for Project Winfield portal access
rootCause: Winfield self-signed root CA certificate not installed in the Trusted Root Certification Authorities store
solution: Windows GUI: Right-click Winfield.cer → Install Certificate → Local Machine → Place in Trusted Root Certification Authorities. Windows PowerShell: Import-Certificate -FilePath 'C:\WinfieldClient\Winfield.cer' -CertStoreLocation 'Cert:\LocalMachine\Root' -Verbose. Linux (Ubuntu): sudo cp ~/WinfieldClient/winfield.cer /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/AzureStackCertificateAuthority.crt && sudo update-ca-certificates
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Winfield portal (https://portal.autonomous.cloud.private/) fails to load in the browser after DNS and certificate are configured
rootCause: Network connectivity issue or service not responding on the Winfield appliance
solution: 1. Verify connectivity: Invoke-WebRequest https://portal.autonomous.cloud.private/api/ping. 2. Ensure using a modern browser (Edge/Chrome, not IE). 3. Log in with provided credentials. 4. Ensure Azure CLI v2.59.0+ is installed. 5. Verify Winfield supporting files (Winfield.cer, cloudConfig.json) are available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Resource providers not registered in Winfield subscription, causing failures when performing operations via the portal or CLI in Azure Local disconnected operations
rootCause: Required resource providers (RPs) are in 'NotRegistered' state in the Winfield subscription
solution: List all providers and register unregistered ones: $providers = az provider list --query "[].{Provider:namespace, RegistrationState:registrationState}" -o json | ConvertFrom-Json; foreach ($provider in $providers) { if ($provider.RegistrationState -ne 'Registered') { az provider register --namespace $provider.Provider } }. Verify in Azure portal → Subscription → Settings → Resource providers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Azure Local disconnected operations IRVM01 VM fails to restore with error 0xC000A002: 'The computed authentication tag did not match the input authentication tag' from Microsoft Virtual TPM Device
rootCause: Virtual TPM device authentication tag mismatch prevents IRVM01 from restoring from Saved state, blocking ASZWorker deployment with Winfield
solution: Step 1: Stop-VM IRVM01 -Force. Step 2: Start-VM IRVM01. This should restore the IRVM to a running state. Diagnose by checking: Get-VM IRVM01 on any seed node. If state is 'Saved' and Start-VM fails with 0xC000A002, apply this mitigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/TSG | Winfield | Azurelocal disconnected VM fails to restore

symptom: After connecting K8S cluster to Winfield, pods in azure-arc namespace are not in running state. clusteridentityoperator logs show DNS resolution failure: 'dial tcp: lookup his.autonomous.cloud.private on 10.43.0.10:53: no such host'
rootCause: K8S cluster CoreDNS does not have entries for Winfield private DNS names (his.autonomous.cloud.private, hosting.autonomous.cloud.private, etc.), causing Azure Arc agent pods to fail DNS resolution
solution: Step 1: SSH into master node, create corefile.yaml with custom CoreDNS ConfigMap adding hosts entries mapping Winfield appliance IP (e.g. 10.0.50.4) to hosting.autonomous.cloud.private and other required endpoints. Step 2: kubectl apply -f corefile.yaml. Step 3: kubectl rollout restart deploy coredns -n kube-system
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Workloads/Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes (k8s)/[TSG] Can't connect K8S cluster to Winfield

symptom: Unable to resolve domain name for Project Winfield portal (portal.autonomous.cloud.private) - ping fails to resolve the hostname to an IP address
rootCause: DNS is not configured to resolve the Winfield private domain names (portal.autonomous.cloud.private, login.autonomous.cloud.private, graph.autonomous.cloud.private) on the client machine.
solution: Add entries to the hosts file mapping the Winfield appliance IP to the private domain names. Windows: edit C:\Windows\system32\drivers\etc\hosts as admin. Linux: edit /etc/hosts. Add lines: <WinfieldIP> portal.autonomous.cloud.private, <WinfieldIP> login.autonomous.cloud.private, <WinfieldIP> graph.autonomous.cloud.private (replace <WinfieldIP> with actual appliance IP, e.g. 10.0.50.4).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Unable to install self-signed certificate (Winfield.cer) on client computer for connecting to Project Winfield portal
rootCause: The Winfield self-signed CA certificate is not installed in the Trusted Root Certification Authorities store on the client machine, causing TLS/SSL trust failures when accessing the portal.
solution: Windows GUI: Right-click Winfield.cer → Install Certificate → Local Machine → Trusted Root Certification Authorities. Windows PowerShell: Import-Certificate -FilePath 'C:\WinfieldClient\Winfield.cer' -CertStoreLocation 'Cert:\LocalMachine\Root' -Verbose; also import to Cert:\CurrentUser\Root. Linux (Ubuntu): sudo cp winfield.cer /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/AzureStackCertificateAuthority.crt && sudo update-ca-certificates.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Unable to access Winfield portal at https://portal.autonomous.cloud.private/ - page fails to load in browser
rootCause: Network connectivity issues between the client and the Winfield appliance, or prerequisites (DNS resolution, certificate trust) not properly configured.
solution: 1) Verify connectivity: Invoke-WebRequest https://portal.autonomous.cloud.private/api/ping. 2) Ensure hosts file has correct entries for Winfield domain names. 3) Ensure Winfield.cer is installed in Trusted Root CA store. 4) Use a modern browser (Edge recommended, IE not supported). 5) Ensure Azure CLI 2.59.0+ is installed. 6) Log in with provided credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Resource providers not registered in Winfield subscription, causing issues with portal functionality and resource operations
rootCause: Required Azure resource providers are not registered in the subscription used by Winfield, preventing resource operations from succeeding.
solution: 1) Open Azure portal → Subscription → Settings → Resource providers to check registration status. 2) Register all unregistered providers via CLI: az provider list --query "[].{Provider:namespace, RegistrationState:registrationState}" -o json, then loop and register each with az provider register --namespace <provider>. 3) Verify all providers show 'Registered' status in the portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/Portal Issues/[TSG] Troubleshoot Connectivity to Project Winfield using the portal

symptom: Azure Local disconnected operations VM (IRVM01) fails to restore with error 0xC000A002 - 'Microsoft Virtual TPM Device: Failed to restore with Error: The computed authentication tag did not match the input authentication tag'
rootCause: The IRVM01 VM's saved state has a corrupted or mismatched TPM authentication tag, causing the Hyper-V Virtual TPM device to fail authentication verification during VM restore from saved state.
solution: Force stop and restart the VM to bypass the corrupted saved state: Step 1: Stop-VM IRVM01 -Force. Step 2: Start-VM IRVM01. This performs a fresh cold boot instead of restoring from the saved state, resolving the TPM authentication mismatch. Found in build Winfield 6.1064548904.13854.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Operations and Management/TSG | Winfield | Azurelocal disconnected VM fails to restore

symptom: Cannot connect Kubernetes cluster to Winfield - pods in azure-arc namespace not running, clusteridentityoperator logs show DNS resolution failure: 'lookup his.autonomous.cloud.private on 10.43.0.10:53: no such host'
rootCause: CoreDNS in the K8S cluster does not have host entries for Winfield's private domain names (his.autonomous.cloud.private, hosting.autonomous.cloud.private, etc.), so DNS queries for these domains fail from within the cluster.
solution: Update CoreDNS ConfigMap to add Winfield host entries: 1) Create corefile.yaml with a ConfigMap that includes a 'hosts' block mapping the Winfield IP (e.g. 10.0.50.4) to all required Winfield domains (hosting.autonomous.cloud.private, etc.). 2) kubectl apply -f corefile.yaml. 3) kubectl rollout restart deploy coredns -n kube-system. This enables K8S pods to resolve Winfield endpoints internally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Workloads/Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes (k8s)/[TSG] Can't connect K8S cluster to Winfield

symptom: Running OnboardingScript.sh to connect Linux server to Winfield (Azure Local Disconnected Operations) fails with certificate error: 'cannot verify artifacts.blob.autonomous.cloud.private's certificate - Unable to locally verify the issuer's authority'. Certificate cannot be installed to Linux root CA store via update-ca-certificates (shows '0 added').
rootCause: The certificate exported by Export-WinfieldRootCert is not compatible with Linux. The command exports additional data that Linux does not accept (file contains more than one certificate). Bug raised with PG.
solution: 1) On a Windows machine with the cert installed, open certlm.msc → Trusted Root Certificate Authorities → Certificates. 2) Right-click the AzureStackCertificationAuthority cert → All Tasks → Export → select 'Base-64 encoded X.509 (.CER)' format → save as Winfieldbase64.cer. 3) Copy Winfieldbase64.cer to Linux VM (e.g. via WinSCP). 4) Run: sudo cp Winfieldbase64.cer /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/Winfieldbase64.crt && sudo update-ca-certificates. Should show '1 added'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Workloads/Azure Arc-enabled servers/[TSG] Can't connect Linux server to Winfield with certificate error

symptom: Invoke-AzStackHciArcInitialization bootstrap process hangs for ~45 minutes then fails on Azure Local Disconnected Operations (ALDO) build 2602.2.25259. Bootstrap service fails during arc initialization step.
rootCause: CRL (Certificate Revocation List) check in the BootstrapManagementService blocks bootstrap completion on build 2602.2.25259.
solution: Run PowerShell script on all nodes PRIOR to arc initialization: 1) Find config file at C:\windows\system32\bootstrap\content_*\Microsoft.Azure.Edge.Bootstrap.ManagementService\windows.mae.config.json. 2) Set ManagementSettings.CheckCertificateRevocationList = $false. 3) Stop-Service BootstrapManagementService. 4) Save modified config. 5) Start-Service BootstrapManagementService and wait 60s. Full script available in ADO wiki Known Issues #181444.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Known Issues/181444 - ALDO - bootstrap fails on 2602.2.25259

symptom: Linux server fails to connect to Winfield (Azure Local disconnected operations) with certificate error: 'Unable to locally verify the issuer's authority' when running OnboardingScript.sh for Arc-enabled server onboarding. The cert exported by Export-WinfieldRootCert cannot be installed via update-ca-certificates (shows '0 added').
rootCause: Export-WinfieldRootCert exports the certificate in a format incompatible with Linux — the exported file contains additional data (multiple certificates) that Linux does not accept. The cert must be re-exported as Base-64 encoded X.509 format.
solution: 1) On Windows (e.g. Hyper-V host), open certlm.msc → Trusted Root CAs → find AzureStackCertificationAuthority cert. 2) Right-click → Export → select 'Base-64 encoded X.509 (.CER)' format → save as Winfieldbase64.cer. 3) Copy to Linux VM. 4) Run: sudo cp Winfieldbase64.cer /usr/local/share/ca-certificates/Winfieldbase64.crt && sudo update-ca-certificates (should show '1 added').
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Customer Scenarios/Workloads/Azure Arc-enabled servers/[TSG] Can't connect Linux server to Winfield with certificate error

symptom: ALDO bootstrap fails on build 2602.2.25259 - Invoke-AzStackHciArcInitialization hangs for ~45 minutes then fails during disconnected operations deployment.
rootCause: CRL (Certificate Revocation List) check in the BootstrapManagementService blocks arc initialization, causing the bootstrap process to hang and eventually timeout.
solution: Run PowerShell script on all nodes PRIOR to arc initialization: locate windows.mae.config.json under C:\windows\system32 ootstrap\content_*\Microsoft.Azure.Edge.Bootstrap.ManagementService, set CheckCertificateRevocationList to false, restart BootstrapManagementService, wait 60 seconds. Full script in ADO wiki.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Known Issues/181444 - ALDO - bootstrap fails on 2602.2.25259

symptom: Azure Local (ALDO) deployment validation fails after creating KeyVault in the ALDO portal. Error occurs if validation is started within 30 minutes of KeyVault creation.
rootCause: RBAC permissions for KeyVault require 30+ minutes to fully propagate after creation. Starting validation before this period triggers a permission-related validation failure.
solution: Wait at least 30 minutes after creating and configuring the KeyVault before starting the deployment validation step. This is a known upstream bug on the backlog.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Labs for ALDO CSS/Single Node/Azure Local Deployment

symptom: ALDO deployment validation fails after creating KeyVault in the disconnected operations portal. Error occurs if validation is started within 30 minutes of KeyVault creation.
rootCause: RBAC permissions for KeyVault require 30+ minutes to fully propagate after creation. Starting validation before this period triggers a permission-related validation failure.
solution: Wait at least 30 minutes after creating and configuring the KeyVault before starting the deployment validation step. This is a known upstream bug on the backlog.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Labs for ALDO CSS/Single Node/Azure Local Deployment

symptom: ALDO single-node lab menu system throws an error and exits when running 'configure physical host for new virtual environment' option (Advanced > option 6). Appears as deployment failure.
rootCause: Known bug in the menu system's physical host configuration command. The error is cosmetic and does not affect deployment.
solution: The error can be safely ignored. Restart the menu system by re-running Invoke-MenuSystem and continue with the DC and node deployment steps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Labs for ALDO CSS/Single Node/VM Deployment virtual

symptom: ALDO VM deployment uses different data disk scripts for ALCSS01-10 vs ALCSS17-20 environments - using the wrong script causes deployment failure
rootCause: ALCSS01-10 environments use an updated data disk with a data stripe created in WinPE (Enable-Dynamic-SSD-Disks), while ALCSS17-20 (original ASZCSS10A-D nodes) use the legacy Create-Data-Disk script.
solution: For ALCSS01-10: use the Enable-Dynamic-SSD-Disks template script. For ALCSS17-20: use the original Create-Data-Disk template script. Check the physical node name prefix to determine the correct script.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Labs for ALDO CSS/Single Node/VM Deployment virtual

symptom: ALCSS23 ALDO environment deployment blocked after redeployment on GA release (version 2602.1.25259 / 12.2602.0.3018)
rootCause: Deployment blocker tracked as Bug 37002200 in msazure DevOps. Specific to GA release version on ALCSS23 B88 environment.
solution: Track Bug 37002200 (msazure.visualstudio.com/One) for resolution status. Consider using alternative long-haul environment (ALCSS17) until fix is available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Multi-node-Long-haul-ALDO-labs

symptom: ALM (Agent Lifecycle Manager) fails to deploy agents during Azure Local deployment. ECE action plan reports agent deployment failure after 25-minute timeout waiting for SuccessIndication file.
rootCause: ALM reads trigger files from ECE to deploy agents in categories (bootstrap, ECE agent, all agents). If trigger file is missing, agent nuget download fails, or agent installation fails, ALM produces a Results file with errors instead of SuccessIndication file in C:\Agents\Orchestration\AgentLifecycleManagement\SuccessIndication*.
solution: 1) Check C:\Agents\Orchestration\AgentLifecycleManagement\SuccessIndication* for success file. 2) If absent, check Results file for specific errors. 3) Verify trigger files exist with correct agent categories. 4) Check nuget store connectivity. 5) To manually trigger ALM: see How to trigger ALM wiki page. 6) ECE waits 25 min - if ALM completes after timeout, manual retry may be needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Agent Lifecycle Management Agent

symptom: wssdcloudagent service appears stopped on some Azure Local (HCI) cluster nodes, causing concern about MOC cloud agent health
rootCause: wssdcloudagent is a clustered service (Failover Cluster generic service role) that runs on only one active node at a time. It is expected to show as 'Stopped' on non-owner nodes.
solution: Verify cluster resource ownership: Get-ClusterResource | ? {$_.Name -like '*moc*'} | Format-Table Name, State, OwnerGroup, OwnerNode. The service should be 'Online' on exactly one owner node. If it shows 'Failed' or 'Offline' on all nodes, check failover cluster health and MOC logs at $(Get-MocConfig).cloudconfiglocation\log\wssdcloudagent.log
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Azure Local MocARB/MOC (Microsoft On-premise Cloud)/MOC Troubleshooting

symptom: Customer attempts virtual/nested deployment of Azure Local Disconnected Operations (ALDO) and encounters failures or unsupported configuration errors
rootCause: Virtual deployments are not supported for Azure Local (ALDO). Only physical hardware is allowed. Minimum requirements: 3 physical nodes, 64GB RAM/node, 24 physical CPU cores/node, 2TB SSD/NVME storage/node.
solution: Inform customer that ALDO requires physical hardware deployment only. Virtual/nested Hyper-V deployments are unsupported. Refer to hardware requirements: 3 nodes min, 64GB RAM, 24 cores, 2TB SSD/NVME per node, 480GB boot drive. Integration requires ADFS on Windows Server 2022 for OIDC, LDAP for group sync, and Active Directory for RBAC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Infrastructure Role Virtual Machine (IR VM)

symptom: Azure Arc Resource Bridge: telemetry-manager pod in CrashLoopBackOff with thousands of restarts in azstackhci-operator-system namespace
rootCause: msi-adapter container inside telemetry-manager pod fails to start or crashes repeatedly, often due to managed identity token acquisition failure or KVA appliance connectivity issues
solution: 1) Copy kubeconfig from Azure Local node to MGMT VM. 2) kubectl describe po <telemetry-manager-pod> -n azstackhci-operator-system. 3) kubectl logs <pod> -n azstackhci-operator-system -c msi-adapter --tail=100. 4) Check --previous logs if container keeps restarting. 5) Verify ARB VM network connectivity and MSI token endpoint access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Azure Local MocARB/Azure Arc Resource Bridge/Azure Arc Resource Bridge Troubleshooting

symptom: IRVM (Winfield appliance) VM disconnected or offline, unable to upload diagnostic data in ALDO environment
rootCause: IRVM is a black-box clustered VM in air-gapped environments; when VM fails or loses connectivity, standard diagnostic upload paths are unavailable
solution: Use fallback logging process: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureAdaptiveCloud/_wiki/wikis/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki/1623842/Fallback-Logging. All diagnostics must use external logs, telemetry, and supported interfaces. CSS cannot crack open IRVM internals.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Infrastructure Role Virtual Machine (IR VM)

symptom: MOC Cloud Agent (wssdcloudagent) or Node Agent (wssdagent) not running on Azure Local cluster nodes; MOC operations failing
rootCause: wssdcloudagent is clustered (runs on one node only - expected). If wssdagent stopped on any node, indicates node-level MOC failure. Panic traces in logs indicate agent crashes.
solution: 1) Get-Service wssdcloudagent/wssdagent on all nodes. 2) Get-ClusterResource | ? Name -like *moc* to check ownership. 3) Check logs: $(Get-MocConfig).cloudconfiglocation\log\wssdcloudagent.log and C:\ProgramData\wssdagent\log\wssdagent.log. 4) Look for panic: traces. 5) Collect: Get-MocLogs -Path C:\moclogs -Verbose. 6) Filter events with Get-MocEventLog.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Azure Local MocARB/MOC (Microsoft On-premise Cloud)/MOC Troubleshooting

symptom: Add-Server or cluster node addition fails in Azure Stack HCI / Azure Local due to ComponentID mismatch in Network ATC - ValidateAzureStackNetworkATCSettings errors appear in logs
rootCause: Mismatch between network intent and adapter IDs (ComponentID) on the new node vs existing cluster nodes, causing Network ATC provisioning validation to fail
solution: Check logs for ValidateAzureStackNetworkATCSettings errors. Identify the ComponentID mismatch between intent and adapter IDs. Apply mitigation scripts to correct the ComponentID mapping before retrying Add-Server. Ref: Azure Local deployment and management wiki - TSG Network ATC provisioning fails due to ComponentID mismatch
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Node Management/Add Node

symptom: During or after node addition in Azure Stack, background storage repair/optimization jobs do not auto-retry and remain stuck
rootCause: Azure Stack background storage jobs may not automatically retry after failure during node operations
solution: Manually trigger storage repair via PowerShell (Repair-AzsScaleUnitNode) or Azure Stack Admin Portal. Monitor storage jobs with Get-StorageJob to confirm completion before proceeding with further cluster operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Node Management/Add Node

symptom: Suspend-ClusterNode fails with error 'Currently unsafe to perform the operation' when attempting to drain an Azure Local node for maintenance
rootCause: Cluster health conditions prevent safe drain - possible causes include unhealthy storage volumes, running storage jobs, degraded virtual disks, or VMs in failed/paused state that cannot be live-migrated
solution: Before draining: (1) Verify all nodes are Up with Get-ClusterNode, (2) Check CSVs are Online with Get-ClusterSharedVolume, (3) Verify virtual disks are healthy with Get-VirtualDisk, (4) Wait for storage jobs to complete with Get-StorageJob, (5) Check VM status across all nodes. Resolve any unhealthy conditions before retrying Suspend-ClusterNode -Name <node> -Drain.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Node Management/Suspend & Resume/Draining and Resuming an Azure Local Node

symptom: Kerberos authentication fails with KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN error in Azure Local/SDN environment, falling back to less secure NTLM protocol
rootCause: Service Principal Names (SPNs) are misconfigured, missing, or duplicated - Kerberos cannot locate the service account and falls back to NTLM
solution: Use 'setspn -L <account>' to list current SPNs and verify correctness. Use 'setspn -S <SPN> <account>' to register missing SPNs. Check for duplicates with 'setspn -X'. Ensure all systems trust the same KDC and have synchronized time. For SQL Server deployments, use Kerberos Configuration Manager to diagnose SPN issues. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-local/manage/kerberos-with-spn
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/SDN Overview/Manage SDN Security/Authenticate with Kerberos

symptom: Add-Server or node addition fails in Azure Stack HCI / Azure Local due to ComponentID mismatch between Network ATC intent and adapter IDs
rootCause: ComponentID mismatch between the Network ATC intent configuration and the physical adapter identifiers on the new node, causing validation failures during Add-Server
solution: Check adapter ComponentIDs via Get-NetAdapter, compare with existing cluster intent. If mismatched, update the intent or adapter configuration. Check ValidateAzureStackNetworkATCSettings logs for specific errors
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Node Management/Add Node

symptom: Network ATC provisioning fails during Add-Server or Update operations with ValidateAzureStackNetworkATCSettings errors
rootCause: Network ATC settings validation fails due to misconfigured intents, adapter mismatches, or incompatible network configuration on the new node
solution: Validate logs for ValidateAzureStackNetworkATCSettings errors. Apply mitigation scripts to fix adapter-intent alignment. Ensure the new node network configuration matches the cluster baseline
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Node Management/Add Node

symptom: Storage background jobs are not auto-retried after Add Node operation in Azure Stack, requiring manual intervention
rootCause: Azure Stack background storage jobs (repair, optimization) may not automatically retry after node addition, leaving storage in inconsistent state
solution: Manually repair via PowerShell (Repair-AzsScaleUnitNode) or Admin Portal. Check storage jobs with Get-StorageJob. Use Get-AzureStackLog to collect ECE, FabricRingServices, PXE, SeedRingServices, Storage, StorageController logs for diagnosis
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/Node Management/Add Node

symptom: Kerberos authentication fails and falls back to less secure NTLM protocol in Azure Local / SDN environments; KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN errors
rootCause: Service Principal Names (SPNs) are misconfigured, missing, or duplicated, causing Kerberos to fail SPN lookup and fall back to NTLM
solution: Use setspn tool to register and verify SPNs for services. Check for duplicate SPNs (setspn -X). Ensure all participating systems trust the same KDC and have synchronized time. Monitor logs for KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN. Use Kerberos Configuration Manager for SQL Server to troubleshoot SPN issues
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/SDN Overview/Manage SDN Security/Authenticate with Kerberos

symptom: SDN deployment or management conflicts on Azure Local when mixing management methods (on-premises tools WAC/SDN Express vs Azure Arc Add-EceFeature)
rootCause: Two SDN management methods are mutually exclusive. Running Add-EceFeature on a cluster deployed with on-premises tools (or vice versa) causes conflicts — each expects exclusive Network Controller control and programs different VM types.
solution: Choose one method exclusively: (1) Add-EceFeature -> manage via Azure portal/CLI/ARM only, NSG scope = Azure Local VMs. (2) On-premises tools -> manage via WAC/PowerShell/SCVMM only, NSG scope = Hyper-V/SCVMM VMs. Never mix on same cluster.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/SDN Overview/SDN in progress/SDN in Azure Local overview

symptom: SDN deployment conflict when mixing management methods on Azure Local: using both Add-EceFeature (Arc) and on-premises tools (WAC/SDN Express) on the same cluster causes failures or undefined behavior
rootCause: Azure Local SDN has two mutually exclusive methods: SDN enabled by Azure Arc (Add-EceFeature, failover clustered NC) vs SDN managed by on-premises tools (WAC/SDN Express, service fabric NC). Mixing them creates conflicting Network Controller configurations.
solution: Use only one SDN management method per cluster. If NC deployed via Add-EceFeature, do NOT use WAC/SDN Express. If NC deployed via on-premises tools, do NOT run Add-EceFeature. Verify deployment method before making SDN changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/SDN Overview/SDN in progress/SDN in Azure Local overview

symptom: Start-SdnDataCollection shows warnings about inability to copy files using port 445 (SMB) during SDN diagnostic log collection on Azure Local DC
rootCause: Port 445 (SMB) may be blocked between the Domain Controller and target SDN nodes, preventing direct file copy during diagnostic data collection
solution: Ignore port 445 warnings. SdnDiagnostics module automatically falls back to WinRM for file copy. Collected files appear at C:\Windows\Tracing\SdnDiag\SdnDataCollection_<datetime>. Upload to Helen workspace for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/SDN Overview/SDN in progress/SDN Log Collection - SDN Managed by on-premise tools

symptom: Need to capture SDN network traces for troubleshooting virtual networking issues in Azure Local (ALDO)
solution: Use Start-SdnNetshTrace from SdnDiagnostics module to capture traces (e.g., Start-SdnNetshTrace -ComputerName 'machine01','machine02' -Role:Server), reproduce the issue, then Stop-SdnNetshTrace. Use Start-SdnDataCollection to automatically collect traces. Copy to on-prem file server for analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/SDN Overview/SDN in progress/SDN network trace

symptom: Cannot collect diagnostic logs in Azure Local disconnected operations because the appliance VM is down or the management endpoint is inaccessible
rootCause: Appliance VM unavailable or management endpoint unreachable, preventing standard log collection methods
solution: Use Fallback Log Collection: manually extract logs from mounted VHDs using Copy-DiagnosticData script, then send to Microsoft using Send-DiagnosticData script.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Observability/Fallback Log Collection

symptom: Need to collect diagnostic logs in Azure Local disconnected operations when appliance is connected to Azure and management endpoint is accessible
solution: Use Direct Collection: trigger Invoke-ApplianceLogCollection from the management endpoint. This is the simplest method when the appliance has Azure connectivity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Observability/On-Demand Log Collection

symptom: Need to collect diagnostic logs in Azure Local when the appliance is disconnected from Azure but the management endpoint is still accessible
rootCause: Appliance disconnected from Azure, cannot use direct collection method
solution: Use Indirect Collection: trigger Invoke-ApplianceLogCollectionAndSaveToShareFolder to save logs to a local share, then use Send-DiagnosticData to transmit logs to Microsoft when connectivity is available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Observability/On-Demand Log Collection

symptom: Error when running `az aksarc nodepool add` with invalid --os-sku value or --os-type/--os-sku mismatch on AKS enabled by Azure Arc (Azure Local disconnected)
rootCause: Invalid --os-sku parameter value specified, or mismatch between --os-type and --os-sku parameters
solution: Use valid --os-sku values: 'CBLMariner' (for --os-type Linux), 'Windows2019' or 'Windows2022' (for --os-type Windows). Ensure --os-type matches the os-sku.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Workloads/AKS enabled by Azure Arc/03-Scale and add Pool AKS

symptom: Cannot get ACR credentials or pull private container images in Azure Local disconnected environment — `az acr credential show` or `az acr login` fails
rootCause: ACR admin user is not enabled by default; Kubernetes imagePullSecrets require admin credentials for private ACR in disconnected environments
solution: Enable admin access: `az acr update -n <acrName> --admin-enabled true`, then retrieve credentials with `az acr credential show --name <acrName>`. Create Kubernetes docker-registry secret using ACR login server, username and password for image pulling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Workloads/AKS enabled by Azure Arc/05-Deploying Private ACR Image to AKS

symptom: Need to capture SDN network traces for troubleshooting Azure Local SDN issues (e.g., virtual gateway L3/GRE/IPSec connectivity problems)
solution: Use SdnDiagnostics module: 1) Start-SdnNetshTrace -ComputerName 'hosts' -Role:Server, 2) Reproduce the network scenario, 3) Stop-SdnNetshTrace, 4) Copy traces to on-prem file server or run Start-SdnDataCollection to auto-collect. Ref: https://github.com/microsoft/SdnDiagnostics/wiki/Start-SdnNetshTrace
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Infrastructure/SDN Overview/SDN in progress/SDN network trace

symptom: Cannot collect diagnostic logs in Azure Local disconnected operations (ALDO) when appliance VM is down or management endpoint is inaccessible
rootCause: Standard log collection mechanisms (direct/indirect) require the appliance VM and management endpoint to be operational; when both are unavailable, no built-in collection path exists
solution: Use fallback log collection: 1) Extract logs from mounted VHDs using Copy-DiagnosticData script, 2) Send extracted logs to Microsoft via Send-DiagnosticData script. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-local/manage/disconnected-operations-fallback
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Observability/Fallback Log Collection

symptom: Container exits immediately after starting in Azure Local Disconnected Operations environment
rootCause: Container Entry Point (CEP) script fails or exits early during initialization
solution: Check logs under /var/log/cep.log or container stdout. Ensure CEP scripts are idempotent and resilient to restarts. Validate that all required binaries and config files exist at the expected paths.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Workloads/Container Entry Point (CEP) in Azure Local Disconnected Operations

symptom: Services (azshostagent, azsnode-s) not starting inside Azure Local disconnected container
rootCause: Missing binaries or configuration files in the container image for CEP services
solution: Validate paths and permissions in the CEP script. Ensure required binaries (/usr/local/bin/azshostagent, /usr/local/bin/azsnode-s) exist with correct permissions. Check that config files (e.g., /etc/azs/hostagent.json) are properly mounted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Workloads/Container Entry Point (CEP) in Azure Local Disconnected Operations

symptom: Health probes failing for container in Azure Local Disconnected Operations
rootCause: CEP did not start all required background services before the container was marked as ready
solution: Ensure all background processes (azshostagent, azsnode-s, etc.) are launched and monitored in the CEP script. Use 'wait' command to keep the entrypoint alive. Validate that all services are running before marking the container as healthy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Disconnected Operations/Readiness/Workloads/Container Entry Point (CEP) in Azure Local Disconnected Operations

symptom: rx-missed packet drops on Azure Operator Nexus tenant workload VMs running latency-sensitive dataplane workloads
rootCause: CPU stalls preventing timely RX ring draining due to missing CPU isolation (isolcpus/nohz_full/rcu_nocbs) and kubelet CPUAffinity not restricted in tenant VM
solution: Configure kernel boot parameters: isolcpus, nohz_full, rcu_nocbs for dataplane CPUs. Set kubelet CPUAffinity by creating /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d/10-cpu-affinity.conf with [Service] CPUAffinity=0 1, then restart node. This is tenant-managed, not a Nexus platform defect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Components/CPU Isolation and Affinity in Azure Operator Nexus

symptom: ingress_overload or similar dataplane drops on Azure Operator Nexus tenant VMs, with regular millisecond-scale stalls observed in traces
rootCause: Longer kernel stalls from shared CPU scheduling without isolation; scheduler ticks and RCU callbacks interrupt dataplane threads
solution: Add kernel boot params: isolcpus=<dataplane-cpus> nohz_full=<dataplane-cpus> rcu_nocbs=<dataplane-cpus>. Suppresses scheduler tick, removes CPUs from normal scheduling, offloads RCU callbacks. Requires node reboot. Validate with: cat /proc/cmdline.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Components/CPU Isolation and Affinity in Azure Operator Nexus

symptom: kubelet housekeeping preempts dataplane threads on Nexus tenant VM, causing intermittent performance degradation under load
rootCause: kubelet CPUAffinity not set - by default kubelet runs on all CPUs including dataplane cores
solution: Create kubelet CPU affinity drop-in: sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service.d && create 10-cpu-affinity.conf with [Service] CPUAffinity=0 1. Run systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl restart kubelet. Verify with: systemctl show -p CPUAffinity kubelet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Components/CPU Isolation and Affinity in Azure Operator Nexus

symptom: NAKS (Nexus AKS) log collector script cannot be operated or fails to run on the cluster; customer unable to collect diagnostic logs required for IcM investigation
rootCause: Collector script may fail due to environment-specific issues on NAKS cluster; script execution depends on cluster accessibility and proper node connectivity
solution: 1) SSH to Control Plane node via az connectedk8s proxy or direct SSH. 2) Manually export cloud-init logs: /var/log/cloud-init.log and /var/log/cloud-init-output.log. 3) If customer cannot copy logs via standard method, create az ssh config with az ssh config --subscription <subId> --local-user azureuser --private-key-file <file> --resource-group <rg> --name <name> --resource-type Microsoft.HybridCompute/machines --port 22 -f <ssh-config-file> then use scp -O -F <ssh-config-file> <path>/node_name_date.tar.gz to copy files
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/Exporting the logs when collector script cannot be operated

symptom: Azure Operator Nexus VM lifecycle operations (e.g. DELETE) fail with HTTP 5xx errors; K8BridgeProxyTraces show TLS handshake timeout or failed to establish secure connection messages; operations fail intermittently but succeed on retry
rootCause: Transient TLS handshake failures caused by: 1) control plane load/concurrency exhausting connection/thread/CPU pools, 2) network-level packet loss or latency spikes, 3) downstream component restarts or rolling updates, 4) certificate chain validation latency
solution: 1) Retry the operation - single TLS handshake timeouts are expected transient phenomena by design. 2) Correlate across ARM -> RPaaS -> NetworkCloud -> Proxy layers. 3) Check K8BridgeProxyTraces for TLS handshake timeout. 4) If retry succeeds, benign. 5) Escalate only if repeated failures across multiple customers/clusters or sustained control-plane unavailability
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/Transient TLS Handshake Errors

symptom: Azure Operator Nexus network device metrics (e.g. Lldp Tlv Discards) show no data / empty graph in Azure Monitor across all devices; other metrics like CPU and Memory flow normally
rootCause: Counter-based metrics only emit when the underlying counter increments; no zero values emitted. When no relevant events occur, metric produces no data points. By design, not telemetry failure. Known doc gap raised as repair item (ref IcM 21000000919452)
solution: Explain to customer that no data is expected behavior for counter-based metrics when no events occur. Verify other metrics flow normally to confirm telemetry pipeline is healthy. Reference SR 2602120040006666 and IcM 21000000919452
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/Understanding Counter-Based Metrics in Azure Operator Nexus

symptom: During NF wipe and repave, instance appears active in Azure portal despite being in maintenance mode internally. Portal does not reflect actual internal state transitions.
rootCause: Internal state transitions for Network Fabric instances are not exposed in the Azure portal UI. This is a known limitation.
solution: 1) Use internal tools (Fabric Control Plane or Instance State Viewer) to verify actual state. 2) Cross-check with telemetry logs. 3) Notify customer portal may not reflect current state. 4) Document discrepancy. 5) File low-priority ICM but proceed if internal state is valid.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/Wipe and Repave Procedure for NF Instances

symptom: L2 network creation fails with errors related to Kubernetes cluster connectivity during NF (Network Fabric) wipe and repave process.
rootCause: Hybrid connection agent is not connected or the NAKS cluster is unhealthy, preventing L2 network provisioning.
solution: 1) Identify target NAKS cluster from instance metadata. 2) Run kubectl get nodes and kubectl get pods -A. 3) Check hybrid connection agent status, restart if unresponsive. 4) Validate ports and DNS. 5) Retry L2 network creation. 6) If persists, collect logs and escalate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/Wipe and Repave Procedure for NF Instances

symptom: CMBU fails when invalid or non-existent target version is specified for Geneva Action; Cluster Manager enters failed state with 'Selected version does not exist' in activity log
rootCause: No RP-side validation exists for CMBU target version. Invalid version passes through RP and fails at Cluster Manager level.
solution: Validate target version before triggering Geneva Action. If failed, retry with correct/valid version to resume the update.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[Doc] Cluster Management Bundle Update (CMBU) Deep Dive

symptom: CMBU update halts after first cluster fails; remaining clusters not updated; concurrency set to 0; cannot proceed without manual intervention
rootCause: CMBU failure propagation logic halts all updates when first cluster fails to prevent cascading failures across the fleet. Concurrency is set to 0.
solution: Resume update using Geneva action: Resume-CMU -ClusterManager <name>. This sets spec.resumeUpdate=true in the cluster CR. Monitor first cluster closely as it determines fleet-wide progression.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[Doc] Cluster Management Bundle Update (CMBU) Deep Dive

symptom: Azure Operator Nexus resources (NFC, Network Fabric, Cluster) not visible in Azure Portal despite being visible via Azure CLI with provisioning state 'Failed'
rootCause: Resources that fail early in provisioning may not complete registration in ARM. Azure Portal only displays resources fully materialized in ARM.
solution: Use Azure CLI to verify provisioning state. Check Activity Logs for delete operations. Review backend logs with correlation IDs. Validate RBAC permissions and propagation timing. If transient, retry creation without deleting the resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[Doc] Why some Azure Resources may not appear in the Azure Portal

symptom: BMM node temporarily cordoned due to transient network instability; PortDownProblem or PortFlappingProblem conditions triggered on single node; node auto-recovers
rootCause: Transient eth0 down on single BMM node causes PortDown/PortFlapping conditions, triggering automatic cordon. Issue is isolated to single node, not cluster-wide.
solution: No action needed for transient events. Verify: BmmNetworkLinksUp stabilized, hwvalidation-failed is false, provisioningState is provisioned, cordonStatus is Uncordoned. Check degradedStartTime/degradedEndTime to confirm self-resolution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[How-To] BMM Network Issue Diagnosis Using Logs and Dashboards

symptom: Engineers misinterpret BMM provisioningState as operational health; provisioningState shows Failed but BMM is fully operational with workloads running normally
rootCause: provisioningState only tracks ARM-initiated PUT/PATCH operations (creation, replace, reimage, tag updates). A failed tag-only update sets provisioningState to Failed without any impact on machine health or readiness.
solution: Use detailedStatus and detailedStatusMessage for operational state assessment. readyState indicates cluster eligibility. Never use provisioningState for operational decisions, troubleshooting, or escalation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[How-To] Understanding readyState in Azure Operator Nexus Bare Metal Machines

symptom: CMBU upgrade from 4.8.5 to 4.9.7 fails; Nexus overlay extension update fails with Helm timeout and rollback; deployment watcher readiness failure due to stale CRDs
rootCause: hybridaksclusters CRD storedVersions contains stale v1 entry conflicting with spec.versions due to mid-migration state, causing Kubernetes to reject the extension update.
solution: Use Geneva Action RetrieveKubernetesResource to check hybridaksclusters CRDs. If storedVersions has v1, use ClusterExecuteScript GA with Lockbox to delete stale CRDs: kubectl delete crd hybridaksclusters.aks.afo-nc.microsoft.com hybridaksclusters.infrastructure.hybridaks.microsoft.com
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[How-To] identify stale HAKS CRDs to preemptively unblock upgrade to CMBU 4.9.7

symptom: IPv6 connectivity fails while IPv4 works in PCT lab environment; issue impacts multiple Nexus clusters and vendors; traffic exits Nexus but never reaches Azure destinations
rootCause: IPv6 was added to VNET after ExpressRoute gateway creation. Gateway did not advertise IPv6 routes until redeployed. Not a Nexus defect.
solution: Route to Azure ExpressRoute - Connectivity and Performance (Private Peering) team. VNET gateway must be redeployed after IPv6 configuration on VNET. CSS should validate Nexus/NAKS health, confirm out-of-scope indicators, then escalate to correct infrastructure owner.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[How-to] Handling PCT Lab Connectivity Issues

symptom: NAKS KubernetesCluster CR has status.terminal=true; nc-aks-operator stops all reconciliation; cluster lifecycle operations make no further progress indefinitely
rootCause: Terminal state is set when nc-aks-operator encounters an unrecoverable condition. Once terminal=true, all reconciliation stops permanently until manually cleared.
solution: Requires DRI action: kubectl -n nc-system patch kubernetescluster <NAME> --subresource=status --type merge --patch with status.terminal set to false. Collect minimum evidence before escalation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[How To] NAKS General Troubleshooting Guide

symptom: NAKS cluster deletion fails because azure-arc-k8sagent feature cannot be removed; clusters stuck in Failed/Deleting provisioning state; error: subscription id is missing or empty from feature variable: global.subscriptionId
rootCause: Bug in feature reconciler triggers deletion before cluster is fully created. When cluster creation fails or is interrupted (often due to capacity issues preventing agent pool node deployment), the subscription ID in ProviderVariables is never populated. Feature deletion logic expects this ID to exist, leaving the cluster stuck.
solution: Manual mitigation: (1) Delete NAKS from portal, (2) Check/delete kubernetescluster CR with kubectl, (3) Patch finalizer on azure-arc-k8sagents feature: kubectl patch kcft -n nc-system <name>-azure-arc-k8sagents --type=json to remove /metadata/finalizers, (4) Wait for NAKS deletion. Fixed in build 2603. Ref Bug 2380334.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Known Issues/156611 - NAKS Cannot Be Deleted azure-arc-k8sagent Feature Failure

symptom: Nexus tenant-level operations (e.g. VM delete) fail with error: net/http: TLS handshake timeout; k8bridgeproxy does not retry after transient TLS/handshake errors
rootCause: k8bridgeproxy limitation: it does not retry on TLS/handshake errors when proxying to k8bridge. Transient backend failures cause TLS handshake timeouts that are not retried.
solution: Retry the failed operation; TLS issues are transient and should succeed on retry. Fix tracked by Bug 2551088. Diagnose via K8BridgeProxyTraces Kusto query filtering level=error, message=HTTP Request Failed, error=TLS handshake timeout.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Known Issues/174897 - K8bridgeproxy doesnt retry after transient TLS handshake errors

symptom: Network Fabric device RefreshConfiguration post-action fails with System.ArgumentNullException: apiVersion cannot be null or whitespace; operation incorrectly allowed when Fabric in Accepted/pending commit state
rootCause: Two related bugs: Bug 2591815 - RefreshConfiguration should be blocked when Fabric is in Accepted (pending commit) state but is allowed. Bug 2591832 - Null exception in PatchArmResource because apiVersion is null during RefreshConfiguration in this state.
