solution: Avoid running az networkfabric device refresh-configuration when Fabric is in Accepted/pending commit state. Complete the commit workflow first. Fixed in NNF 11.0 (management bundle, build 2604).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Known Issues/174926 - Device Refresh Operation allowed when it should be blocked

symptom: After bare metal machine (BMM) replace action, iDRAC interface TLS certificate resets to Dell defaults; users see Dell default certificate when logging into iDRAC
rootCause: Race condition during BMM replace action: hardware validation resets iDRAC (clearing TLS certs) simultaneously with certificate reconciler. Due to race, certificate is replaced BEFORE iDRAC reset, so reset clears the correct alrs-edge-interface-issuer certificate.
solution: Three options: (1) Wait ~40 days for automatic cert rotation (no functional impact), (2) Force cert reinstall via kubectl patch baremetalmachine status to null secretRevision + az networkcloud baremetalmachine run-command, (3) Run BMM restart. Fix in future release.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Known Issues/185376 - Bare metal machine replace actions cause the iDRAC interface TLS certificate to reset to Dell defaults

symptom: cluster-cve-report run-data-extract fails with Failed to retrieve vulnerability report; nexus-trivy-operator shows repeating Reconciler error with unexpected EOF; no new scan jobs; completed scan jobs stuck in nc-system namespace
rootCause: nexus-trivy-operator deadlock: failed scan pod (exit code 1) followed by successful retry, but operator picks failed pod first (no status filtering), gets unexpected EOF, requeues but never deletes stuck scan job. Permanently consumes one of two ConcurrentScanJobsLimit slots. Both consumed = all scanning stops.
solution: See TSG: Scan Job Deadlock - CVE Report Failures at eng.ms for diagnose/fix/verify steps. Present in all pre-NC 4.10 versions. Fixed in NC 4.10 (build 2604).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Known Issues/187018 - Cluster-cve-report fails due to scan job controller deadlock

symptom: BMM Replace operation fails after motherboard replacement when using --storage-policy Preserve. TPM key mismatch error occurs because new motherboard has different TPM key.
rootCause: After motherboard replacement, the TPM key changes. With storage-policy Preserve, the system validates the previous TPM key, causing a mismatch and preventing Replace from completing.
solution: Run BMM Replace with --storage-policy DiscardAll instead of Preserve. Verify by asking customer if motherboard was replaced and if Preserve policy was used, or check onprembmcloudinitlogs for TPM error: WARNING:esys:src/tss2-esys/api/Esys_Load.c:324:Esys_Load_Finish() Received TPM Error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[TSG] Bare Metal Machine (BMM) Replace Troubleshooting Scenarios

symptom: BMM Replace fails with --storage-policy Preserve due to stale BMC password. Authentication fails when system attempts to use old password stored in environment variables.
rootCause: A stale BMC password caused by a failed credential rotation (typically following hardware issues). The system fails authentication with the old password from environment variable $PASSWORD.
solution: Run BMM Replace with --storage-policy DiscardAll. Verify by checking onprembmcloudinitlogs for: Password from environment variable $PASSWORD did not work: Operation not permitted
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[TSG] Bare Metal Machine (BMM) Replace Troubleshooting Scenarios

symptom: Azure CLI run-data-extract command for BMM TSR collection fails with BMMRunDataExtractCMDReconciliationFailed error 4 - Customer script timed out or was killed (137).
rootCause: Default or low --limit-time-seconds value (e.g. 600 seconds) is insufficient for hardware-support-data-collection to complete.
solution: Increase --limit-time-seconds to 1000 or higher. Preferred method: collect TSR via iDRAC9 (Maintenance > SupportAssist > Export SupportAssist Collection). If both methods fail, node responsiveness is the underlying issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[TSG] Collecting TSR from a Bare Metal Machine (BMM)

symptom: Azure Portal Diagnostic Settings blade shows 'The limit of 5 diagnostic settings was reached' but fewer than 5 settings are visible. Cannot create new Diagnostic Settings for Microsoft.NetworkCloud/Clusters resource.
rootCause: Orphaned diagnostic settings referencing deprecated IDRACLogs category remain in resource configuration but are not visible in portal, consuming the 5-setting limit.
solution: Delete orphaned diagnostic settings via REST API (portal cannot delete them): az rest --method delete --url 'https://management.azure.com/<resource-URI>/providers/microsoft.insights/diagnosticSettings/<setting-name>?api-version=2021-05-01-preview'. Collect browser trace (.har) from customer to identify orphaned setting names. After deletion, verify portal displays correct list.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[TSG] Hidden Diagnostic Settings Caused by Deprecated Categories in Nexus Cluster

symptom: NAKS worker nodes show NotReady, stuck in Terminating, or pods stuck in Pending. Kubernetes-level troubleshooting (cordon/drain state, taints, resource constraints) does not explain the behavior.
rootCause: Underlying Bare Metal Machine (BMM) hosting the NAKS node is unhealthy/unreachable/degraded (readyState=False, hardware failure, PXE port unhealthy, or power state mismatch). NAKS nodes run directly on BMMs.
solution: First validate Kubernetes-level causes. If not explanatory: 1) Identify hosting BMM via CSS Nexus Dashboard or Kusto, 2) Inspect BMM health (az networkcloud baremetalmachine show), check readyState/powerState/detailedStatus, 3) If BMM unhealthy, escalate to BMM/Undercloud SRE. Do NOT recommend node deletion while BMM remediation is ongoing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Customer Scenarios/[TSG] Investigating Bare Metal Machine (BMM) Health Impact on NAKS Node Issues

symptom: Network Fabric password rotation fails with connectivity errors; devices remain Out of Sync after rotation attempt. Status shows Out of Sync for affected devices.
rootCause: Transient connectivity failures between Network Fabric Controller and fabric devices during password rotation process. Most common cause of rotation errors.
solution: Built-in retry logic handles transient issues. For persistent failures, trigger resync for failed devices: az networkfabric device resync-password --resource-id <device_resource_id>. Error messages provide actionable next steps. Always attempt resync before opening support cases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Readiness/Technical Overview of Network Fabric Password Rotation Changes

symptom: Network Fabric upgrade is blocked; cannot upgrade fabric to newer version. Upgrade operation fails or is rejected.
rootCause: Fabric upgrades are blocked if any device passwords are Out of Sync after a failed or partial password rotation.
solution: Resync all Out of Sync device passwords before attempting fabric upgrade. Use: az networkfabric device resync-password --resource-id <device_resource_id> for each affected device. Monitor synchronization status until all devices show In Sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Readiness/Technical Overview of Network Fabric Password Rotation Changes

symptom: Network Fabric password rotation API not available or fails on older fabric deployment. Fabric deployed before release 9.2 cannot use new password rotation commands.
rootCause: Fabrics deployed before release 9.2 require one-time migration to the new password rotation scheme (API version 2025-07-15). Legacy secrets need renaming and API field updates.
solution: Run password rotation once to trigger migration. Migration renames secrets and updates API fields automatically. Post-migration, only new API/CLI actions (az networkfabric fabric rotate-password) are supported for password management.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Readiness/Technical Overview of Network Fabric Password Rotation Changes

symptom: NAKS Arc resource shows disconnected or not-connected status; customer cannot manage NAKS cluster from Azure portal/CLI. Arc connectivity lost.
rootCause: Arc connectivity agent (datasyncservice) lost communication with Azure. Various causes including network issues, agent failures, or certificate expiry.
solution: Query K8ConnectRPLogs via ARC Infra Kusto (azarccoreprod.eastus.kusto.windows.net, database K8ConnectRP) to check connectivityStatus timeline. Filter by ServiceName=datasyncservice and ArmId containing subscription/NAKS name. See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-arc-infra-kusto-repo.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Tools and Processes/AON Kusto Repo/ARC Infra Kusto Repo

symptom: ARM operation returns HTTP 403 Forbidden for Nexus/AON resource operations. User or service principal receives authorization denied error.
rootCause: RBAC authorization failure - user or service principal lacks required permissions for the target Nexus resource operation.
solution: Query ARM Traces table (armprodgbl.eastus.kusto.windows.net, database Traces) using correlationId, filter for messages containing Denied or failed access. See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-arm-kusto-repo.md
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Tools and Processes/AON Kusto Repo/ARM Kusto Repo

symptom: Edge browser shows a growing list of expired/outdated PME, AME and GME (Windows Online Services GFS Internal) certificates when authenticating to sensitive sites via YubiKey on SAW, making it difficult to select the correct certificate
rootCause: Certificates are automatically cached by Edge browser each time the user logs into SAW with a YubiKey. Old expired certificates remain in the cache and are never auto-cleaned, causing the certificate picker list to grow indefinitely.
solution: In Edge: Settings > Privacy, Search, and Services > Security > Manage Certificates. Delete all out-of-date PME, AME and Windows Online Services GFS Internal (GME) certificates. They will be automatically re-cached on next SAW login with YubiKey.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Tools and Processes/Clean up old browser certificates

symptom: SAW Edge browser shows too many cached YubiKey certificates (PME, AME, GME) making it difficult to select the correct one for authentication
rootCause: Certificates are automatically uploaded and cached in Edge when logging into SAW with YubiKey. Old expired certificates accumulate over time and are never auto-cleaned.
solution: In Edge: Settings > Privacy, Search, and Services > Security > Manage Certificates > Delete all out-of-date PME, AME, and Windows Online Services GFS Internal (GME) certificates. They will be re-cached automatically when you next log in with YubiKey.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Tools and Processes/Clean up old browser certificates

symptom: Running kubectl ko nbctl lr-list returns error: unknown command ko - Kube-OVN kubectl plugin not installed
rootCause: kubectl ko plugin binary is missing from PATH on this node/jumpbox
solution: Use OVN CLI fallback: kubectl -n kube-system exec -it <ovn-central-pod> -- ovn-nbctl lr-list (and lr-route-list, lr-nat-list) to access OVN NB database directly
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/How to Dump the Routing Table of a Kube-OVN Logical Router

symptom: OVN logical router route exists in lr-route-list but traffic still fails to reach destination in Kube-OVN Custom VPC
rootCause: Router policy (lr-policy-list) drop/reroute conflict, missing/incorrect NAT in lr-nat-list, or data plane not realizing control plane state
solution: Check: 1) lr-route-list prefix/next-hop, 2) lr-policy-list for conflicts, 3) lr-nat-list NAT presence, 4) Data plane with ovn-trace. Compare Vpc.spec.staticRoutes vs OVN NB state.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/How to Dump the Routing Table of a Kube-OVN Logical Router

symptom: IptablesEIP not reachable from outside cluster - all VPC NAT Gateway CRDs show READY=true but EIP does not respond
rootCause: EIP not added as secondary IP on gateway Pod uplink (controller reconciliation error) or upstream router has no ARP/route for EIP subnet
solution: 1) Confirm gateway Pod Running, 2) Verify EIP on Pod uplink: ip addr show, 3) Check iptables rules, 4) Check controller logs for EIP binding errors, 5) Verify upstream provider-network routing
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway - CRD Interactions and Verification

symptom: NAT rule READY=true and iptables rule exists in VPC NAT Gateway Pod but traffic is not being NATed
rootCause: ip_forward disabled inside gateway Pod namespace, earlier ACCEPT rule in FORWARD chain bypasses NAT, or subnet default gateway not pointing to VpcNatGateway lanIp
solution: 1) sysctl net.ipv4.ip_forward check, 2) conntrack -L for flow, 3) iptables rule ordering check, 4) Verify subnet spec.gateway points to VpcNatGateway lanIp
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway - CRD Interactions and Verification

symptom: Non-deterministic NAT, double NAT, or asymmetric traffic in Kube-OVN VPC when both iptables gateway and OVN-native NAT objects exist
rootCause: Mixing iptables-path CRDs (VpcNatGateway/IptablesEIP) and OVN-native CRDs (OvnEip/OvnSnatRule) for same VPC endpoint creates conflicting NAT rules
solution: kubectl get iptables-eip,oeip -o wide | grep <vpc>. Remove all objects from one path. Never mix both paths for same EIP or internal workload.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway - CRD Interactions and Verification

symptom: Kube-OVN IptablesEIP binding fails with pods vpc-nat-gw-xxx not found error in kube-ovn-controller logs
rootCause: Gateway Pod not ready/running when EIP controller tries to bind the IP
solution: Wait for VpcNatGateway Pod to be Running before applying IptablesEIP objects, or delete and re-create the EIP to trigger reconciliation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Manual debug logs collection for a Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway Deployment

symptom: IptablesEIP creation fails with no available ip from external subnet
rootCause: External subnet IP pool exhausted or subnet misconfigured
solution: Check IP pool status: kubectl get subnet <subnet-name> -o jsonpath=.status. Expand subnet CIDR or release unused EIPs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Manual debug logs collection for a Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway Deployment

symptom: IptablesSnatRule fails with iptables: No chain/target/match by that name in kube-ovn-controller logs
rootCause: Referenced IptablesEIP is not yet in READY state when SNAT rule tries to apply
solution: Check EIP status: kubectl get iptables-eips <eip-name> -o wide. Ensure EIP is READY before applying SNAT/DNAT/FIP rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Manual debug logs collection for a Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway Deployment

symptom: Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway Pod in CrashLoopBackOff with panic: interface conversion
rootCause: Kube-OVN version mismatch between controller and gateway Pod image
solution: Ensure controller and gateway Pod images are from the same Kube-OVN release
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Manual debug logs collection for a Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway Deployment

symptom: Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway Pod fails with cant initialize iptables table nat or address already in use
rootCause: Required kernel modules (ip_tables, nf_nat) missing on node, or stale EIP allocation from previous Pod
solution: Load kernel modules: modprobe ip_tables nf_nat. For stale allocation: clean up stale Pod and re-create the gateway.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/Manual debug logs collection for a Kube-OVN VPC NAT Gateway Deployment

symptom: Kube-OVN SecurityGroup allows traffic but connection is dropped by ACL due to priority inversion
rootCause: A broad drop ACL at higher priority shadows the specific allow rule. OVN evaluates ACLs by descending priority.
solution: Ensure specific allow rules have higher priority than broad deny rules. Verify: ovn-nbctl acl-list <port-group> | sort -rn -k2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/SecurityGroup Enforcement & ACL Deep-Dive

symptom: Kube-OVN SecurityGroup rule defined but no corresponding OVN ACL generated
rootCause: kube-ovn-controller has not reconciled the SecurityGroup, or controller is stuck/not running
solution: Check controller logs: kubectl -n kube-system logs deployment/kube-ovn-controller | grep <sg-name>. Restart controller if stuck.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/SecurityGroup Enforcement & ACL Deep-Dive

symptom: Kube-OVN SecurityGroup denies traffic but connection still succeeds despite deny rule
rootCause: Stale conntrack entry from previously allowed connections persists after adding deny rule, or default allow rules (ARP/DHCP) match first
solution: Flush conntrack: ovs-appctl dpctl/flush-conntrack. Check default ACLs: ovn-nbctl acl-list <port-group> | grep -E arp|dhcp|icmp
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/SecurityGroup Enforcement & ACL Deep-Dive

symptom: Kube-OVN SecurityGroup allow rule exists but traffic still blocked in one direction only
rootCause: Rule direction mismatch: ingress rules map to to-lport, egress rules map to from-lport in OVN ACL
solution: Verify ACL direction matches intended traffic flow. ingressRules -> to-lport (into pod), egressRules -> from-lport (out of pod).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/SecurityGroup Enforcement & ACL Deep-Dive

symptom: Kube-OVN SecurityGroup rule silently fails to match any traffic
rootCause: remoteAddress CIDR does not overlap with actual source/destination IP, or uses wrong namespace subnet
solution: Compare pod IPs vs ACL: kubectl get pod -o wide then ovn-nbctl acl-list <port-group> | grep ip4. Check namespace subnet: kubectl get subnet -o wide.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/SecurityGroup Enforcement & ACL Deep-Dive

symptom: Kube-OVN GENEVE tunnel not established between nodes, no cross-node pod connectivity
rootCause: Wrong encap type configured in OVN Southbound DB (e.g., vxlan instead of geneve)
solution: Verify encap type: ovn-sbctl list encap. Ensure all chassis records show type=geneve.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/[In Progress] GENEVE Tunnel Establishment & Encapsulation Logic

symptom: Kube-OVN GENEVE tunnel not established, tcpdump shows no UDP/6081 traffic between nodes
rootCause: Network firewall or host iptables rules blocking UDP port 6081 between cluster nodes
solution: Check firewall rules on all nodes. Test UDP/6081: nc -uz <remote-ip> 6081. Add firewall exception if needed. Check MTU (overlay adds ~50 bytes).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/[In Progress] GENEVE Tunnel Establishment & Encapsulation Logic

symptom: Kube-OVN cluster networking intermittently broken across some node pairs, OVN SB DB inconsistent
rootCause: OVN control plane split-brain due to quorum loss in Southbound DB cluster
solution: Check cluster status: ovn-appctl -t /var/run/ovn/ovnsb_db.ctl cluster/status OVN_Southbound. Restore quorum. Restart ovn-controller on affected nodes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/ALRS Kube-OVN/[In Progress] GENEVE Tunnel Establishment & Encapsulation Logic

symptom: When creating Windows VMs on Azure Local Rack Scale via az stack-hci-vm create using the winmulti-11-qcow2 image, SSH/RDP with the admin-username specified at creation time fails with Permission denied
rootCause: The VM image does not create an account matching the username provided via the admin-username parameter
solution: Use Administrator or azureuser as the username with the password specified via admin-password parameter at creation time
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/Azure Local Rack Scale (ALRS) [aka AL multi-rack]  beta testing effort

symptom: After creating Azure Local VNET subnets, they are not visible in Azure Portal and do not appear in the parent VNET JSON representation
rootCause: Azure Portal does not currently support displaying Azure Local multi-rack VNET subnets
solution: Use Azure CLI commands: az stack-hci-vm network vnet subnet show (if you saved creation values) or az stack-hci-vm network vnet subnet list to query subnets
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/Azure Local Rack Scale (ALRS) [aka AL multi-rack]  beta testing effort

symptom: Linux and Windows VMs on Azure Local Rack Scale are not provisioned with DNS servers specified in the LNET or VNET configuration, causing external DNS name resolution to fail
rootCause: DNS server configuration from LNET/VNET is not propagated to the VM during provisioning
solution: Define DNS configuration at the NIC level before creating VMs, or manually configure the DNS server within the VMs after creation per the OS documentation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/Azure Local Rack Scale (ALRS) [aka AL multi-rack]  beta testing effort

symptom: Creating a Public IP resource on Azure Local Rack Scale fails when the name value contains more than 26 characters or uppercase characters
rootCause: Bug in Public IP resource validation: name field limited to 26 lowercase characters (ADO User Story 2539598)
solution: Use a name value containing 26 or fewer lowercase characters for Public IP resources
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/Azure Local Rack Scale (ALRS) [aka AL multi-rack]  beta testing effort

symptom: Creating NAT gateway inbound rules on Azure Local Rack Scale fails when the name value contains uppercase characters
rootCause: Bug in NAT gateway inbound rules validation: name field cannot contain uppercase characters (ADO User Story 2539469)
solution: Use only lowercase characters in the name field for NAT gateway inbound rules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/Azure Local Rack Scale (ALRS) [aka AL multi-rack]  beta testing effort

symptom: Creating a NAT gateway with inbound rules in a single az stack-hci-vm network nat create command on Azure Local Rack Scale fails due to SDN resource dependency ordering
rootCause: ALRS SDN resources have dependency ordering that prevents inline inbound rules at NAT GW creation time
solution: Follow a 3-step process: (1) Create NAT GW without inbound rules, (2) Associate NAT GW with VNET subnet containing the target NIC resources, (3) Update NAT GW using same create command but now including inbound rules
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Rack Scale/Readiness/Azure Local Rack Scale (ALRS) [aka AL multi-rack]  beta testing effort

symptom: Azure Stack Hub virtual machines are orphaned or ghosted - VMs exist in the system but are not properly managed or accessible
rootCause: VM records become orphaned when the management layer loses track of VMs due to failed operations, interrupted deployments, or database inconsistencies
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIOrphanedVMs from the Azs.Support module to detect and identify orphaned/ghosted VMs on Azure Stack Hub
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Compute/Orphaned_VMs/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIOrphanedVMs

symptom: Azure Stack Hub nodes experienced unexpected reboots - event viewer logs show unplanned restart events
rootCause: Unexpected reboots can be caused by hardware failures, bugcheck/BSOD, firmware issues, or unplanned power events on Azure Stack Hub infrastructure
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIUnexpectedReboot to check event viewer logs. Use -IncludeGracefulShutdowns to include graceful shutdowns, -IncludeInfrastructureVMs to check infra VMs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Compute/Unexpected_Reboot/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIUnexpectedReboot

symptom: Azure Stack Hub cluster nodes have OS activation or licensing issues - activation markers are missing or invalid
rootCause: OS activation markers can become invalid after host reimaging, time sync issues, or KMS connectivity problems on Azure Stack Hub nodes
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIOSActivationMarker to verify OS Activation Marker/License Status across all cluster nodes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Compute/Validate_OS_Activation_Marker/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIOSActivationMarker

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric cluster health is degraded or unhealthy
rootCause: Service Fabric cluster health degradation can result from node failures, replica issues, application errors, or infrastructure component problems
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIServiceFabricClusterHealth to validate the health of Service Fabric clusters in Azure Stack Hub
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Fabric/SF_Health/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIServiceFabricClusterHealth

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric nodes are in disabled state - nodes are not enabled across Fabric Rings
rootCause: Service Fabric nodes can become disabled after failed updates, node repairs, or manual administrative actions that were not properly completed
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIServiceFabricNodesDisabled to check all Fabric Rings for nodes that are not enabled
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Fabric/SF_Node_State/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIServiceFabricNodesDisabled

symptom: Azure Stack Hub C:\HealthAgent directory is missing on infrastructure nodes
rootCause: The C:\HealthAgent directory can be missing after node reimaging or failed health agent installation, preventing health monitoring
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIHealthAgentDirIsMissing to check if C:\HealthAgent exists. Use -Remediate flag to automatically fix
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Health/C_HealthAgent_IsMissing/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIHealthAgentDirIsMissing

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Host Health Agent service is not running or in a failed state on host nodes
rootCause: The Health Agent service may stop or fail to start due to service crashes, configuration corruption, or dependency service failures

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Common.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportNonMaskableInterruptToVM

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infrastructure process is consuming high CPU/memory or appears hung; need to capture memory dump for root cause analysis
solution: Use Invoke-AzsSupportProcDump with -ComputerName and -ProcessId (from Get-AzsSupportService). Default captures full dump (-ma). Use -CustomArguments for advanced: -mp PID -n 3 path
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Common.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportProcDump

symptom: WMI-related issues on Azure Stack Hub infrastructure node (WMI queries timing out, high WmiPrvSE CPU, WMI repository corruption suspected)
solution: Use Invoke-AzsSupportWmiTracing for WMI ETL traces. Specify -ComputerName and -TraceSeconds. Add -IncludeProcDump for concurrent WMI process dumps with -DumpCount and -SecondsBetweenDumps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Common.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportWmiTracing

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infrastructure service is degraded, stuck, or unresponsive and requires restart across multiple nodes safely
solution: Use Restart-AzsSupportService with -Name and -ComputerName. Use -Staggered for rolling restart with -Delay (default 30s). Example: Restart-AzsSupportService -Name NCHostAgent -ComputerName (Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureHost).Name -Staggered
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Common.ps1/Restart-AzsSupportService

symptom: Need to review Azure Stack Hub health validation results (Test-AzureStack) to identify infrastructure warnings or errors
solution: Use Show-AzsSupportValidationSummaryReport to display latest validation results. Filter with -Test (e.g. Alerts), -Warnings, -Errors. Use -Newest N for recent reports, -FilePath for specific file.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Common.ps1/Show-AzsSupportValidationSummaryReport

symptom: Azure Stack Hub has duplicate virtual machine objects that do not align with Hyper-V clustering state
rootCause: VM objects fell out of sync with the cluster, creating duplicate entries visible in Hyper-V but invalid per clustering state
solution: Use Repair-AzsSupportDuplicateVM from Azs.Support Module. Specify -Name with VM GUID or display name. Examples: Repair-AzsSupportDuplicateVM -Name 31393e6b-ee80-4d78-8360-5dadffab0e6b; Repair-AzsSupportDuplicateVM -Name 'AzS-XRP01'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Repair-AzsSupportDuplicateVM

symptom: Azure Stack Hub has orphaned VM objects and stale shared resources that need cleanup
rootCause: VM lifecycle interruption left orphaned objects and shared resources in Hyper-V without proper cleanup
solution: Use Repair-AzsSupportOrphanedVM from Azs.Support Module. Specify target via -ID (VM GUID), -Name (VM name), or -ComputerName (Hyper-V host). Removes orphaned VM objects and associated shared resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Repair-AzsSupportOrphanedVM

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infrastructure VM has missing ACLs on VHDX disk paths, causing access or boot issues
rootCause: ACL permissions on virtual hard disk (VHDX) file paths were lost or corrupted
solution: Use Repair-AzsSupportVirtualMachineACL from Azs.Support Module. Requires -Name (VM name) and -ComputerName (Hyper-V host). Example: Repair-AzsSupportVirtualMachineACL -Name 'AzS-XRP01' -ComputerName 'AzS-S1-N01'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Repair-AzsSupportVirtualMachineACL

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infrastructure VM needs to be refreshed/recovered with proper safeguards and stamp state checks
solution: Use Invoke-AzsSupportVMRefresh. Includes automated safeguards and pre-checks on stamp state before refresh. Params: -ComputerName (case-sensitive), -Role (optional, auto-detected), -Retries (default 3). Example: Invoke-AzsSupportVMRefresh -ComputerName AZS-XRP01 -Role ADFS -Retries 2
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportVMRefresh

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infrastructure VM (e.g., XRP) needs to be safely restarted using action plans
solution: Use Restart-AzsSupportComputer with safe restart action plans. Currently only supports infrastructure VMs. Param: -ComputerName (e.g., Azs-XRP01). Example: Restart-AzsSupportComputer -ComputerName 'Azs-XRP01'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Restart-AzsSupportComputer

symptom: Need to retrieve Azure Stack Hub infrastructure VM objects (ACS, SeedRingServices, FabricRingServices) for diagnostics
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVM from Azs.Support Module. Filter by -Role (ACS, FabricRingServices, etc.). Use -MemoryPressure to check memory state. Examples: Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVM -Role:ACS; Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVM -Role:FabricRingServices -MemoryPressure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVM

symptom: Need to identify which Hyper-V host is running a specific Azure Stack Hub infrastructure VM
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVMHost. Filter by -Role or -VMName. Can pipe from Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVM. Examples: Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVMHost -VMName 'Azs-XRP01'; Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVM -NonHighlyAvailable | Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVMHost
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Get-AzsSupportInfrastructureVMHost

symptom: Need to collect host and infrastructure VM performance metrics on Azure Stack Hub
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportPerformanceMetrics. Internally calls Test-AzureStack -Include AzsInfraPerformance -Debug and returns all host and infrastructure VM performance metrics. Example: Get-AzsSupportPerformanceMetrics
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Get-AzsSupportPerformanceMetrics

symptom: Need a comprehensive VM report (infrastructure + tenant VMs) from Azure Stack Hub hosts
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportVMReport. Returns PSCustomObject; use -OutputPath for CSV export to specified directory (default: AzsSupportWorkingDirectory on ERCS VM). Examples: Get-AzsSupportVMReport | Format-Table; Get-AzsSupportVMReport -OutputPath 'C:\temp\'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Get-AzsSupportVMReport

symptom: Need to query specific virtual machines from Azure Stack Hub Hyper-V hosts by name, ID, state, or host
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportVirtualMachine with filters: -Name, -ID, -ComputerName, -State. Supports -AsJob for background execution with -Wait -PassThru -ExecutionTimeout (default 900s). Examples: Get-AzsSupportVirtualMachine -Name 'AzS-XRP01'; Get-AzsSupportVirtualMachine -ComputerName 'AzS-Node01'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Get-AzsSupportVirtualMachine

symptom: Azure Stack Hub portal persistent disk cache corruption causing portal loading failures, UI errors, or stale content on Admin or Tenant portal
rootCause: Corrupted persistent disk cache files on portal nodes
solution: Run Repair-AzsSupportPortalPersistentDiskCache to delete the cache and restart IIS. Use -Portal "Tenant" or "Admin" to target a specific portal (default: both). Use -CollectData to gather logs for root cause analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.ControlPlane.ps1/Repair-AzsSupportPortalPersistentDiskCache

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infrastructure virtual machines in a specific role (e.g., FabricRingServices, WASPUBLIC, SQL) are unhealthy, stuck, or need safe restart
rootCause: Infrastructure VM role health degradation requiring coordinated restart via safe action plans
solution: Use Restart-AzsSupportComputerByRole -Role:<RoleName> to safely restart all infrastructure VMs in the specified role. Only supports virtual machine roles. Uses ECE safe restart action plans to ensure proper sequencing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Compute.ps1/Restart-AzsSupportComputerByRole

symptom: Azure Stack Hub ECE action plan failed and need to identify the owning engineering team or component for escalation routing
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportRoutingInformation to analyze failed action plans and identify component ownership. Supports: -ActionPlanInstanceId <id>, -MostRecentUpdate (latest update/hotfix AP), -ActionPlanSummaryFilePath <xml-path>, -RolePath/-RoleName for direct component lookup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.EnterpriseCloudEngine.Routing.Info.ps1/Get-AzsSupportRoutingInformation

symptom: Need to check Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric cluster health status across one or all infrastructure rings to diagnose cluster-level issues
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterHealth [-Ring <RingName>] to get aggregated cluster health. Omit -Ring to check all SF rings. Returns PSObject with Ring and native ClusterHealth stats from Get-ServiceFabricClusterHealth.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterHealth

symptom: Need to collect DSC (Desired State Configuration) logs from Azure Stack Hub infrastructure nodes for troubleshooting configuration drift or DSC apply failures
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportDscLogs -ComputerName <NodeName> to collect DSC text and event logs from the specified infrastructure node. Example: Get-AzsSupportDscLogs -ComputerName "Azs-XRP01"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Get-AzsSupportDscLogs

symptom: Need to check Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric cluster upgrade status to troubleshoot stalled or failed cluster upgrades on a specific ring
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterUpgrade -Ring <RingName> (e.g., ACS, FabricRingServices) to get the last cluster upgrade operation status as a PSObject.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterUpgrade

symptom: Need to retrieve Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric cluster configuration JSON for a specific ring for analysis or comparison during troubleshooting
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterConfiguration -Ring <RingName> (e.g., ACS, FabricRingServices) to retrieve the cluster configuration JSON.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterConfiguration

symptom: Need to retrieve Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric cluster manifest XML for a specific ring for troubleshooting or audit
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterManifest -Ring <RingName> (e.g., ACS, FabricRingServices) to retrieve the Service Fabric cluster manifest XML.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricClusterManifest

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric applications, services, partitions, or replicas in unhealthy state; need to identify which components are degraded
rootCause: One or more Service Fabric components (applications, services, partitions, replicas) have entered an unhealthy state
solution: Use Azs.Support module cmdlet: Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricDetail -Ring <RingName> -Unhealthy to list all unhealthy SF components. Ring values from [Enum]::GetValues([SFInfrastructureRole]) e.g. ACS, FabricRingServices.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricDetail

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric primary replica stuck on a node, node being decommissioned, or primary replica needs relocation
rootCause: Primary replica is bound to a specific node that may be unhealthy or undergoing maintenance
solution: Use Move-AzsSupportServiceFabricPrimaryReplica to relocate: Move-AzsSupportServiceFabricPrimaryReplica -Ring <Ring> -ServiceName "fabric:/<App>/<Service>" or -ServiceTypeName "<TypeName>". Supports lookup by ApplicationName+ServiceName or ServiceTypeName.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Move-AzsSupportServiceFabricPrimaryReplica

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Service Fabric primary replica unresponsive or in degraded state for a single-primary-replica service
rootCause: Primary replica process may be hung or in a bad state requiring restart
solution: Use Restart-AzsSupportServiceFabricPrimaryReplica: Restart-AzsSupportServiceFabricPrimaryReplica -Ring <Ring> -ServiceName "fabric:/<App>/<Service>" or -ServiceTypeName "<TypeName>". Only supports services with 1 primary replica.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Restart-AzsSupportServiceFabricPrimaryReplica

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Actors application causing issues in Service Fabric cluster
solution: Remove the Actors application from Service Fabric using: Remove-AzsSupportActorsApplication
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Remove-AzsSupportActorsApplication

symptom: Need to run custom Service Fabric commands on Azure Stack Hub cluster for diagnostics or remediation
solution: Use Invoke-AzsSupportServiceFabricCommand to connect to a SF ring and execute arbitrary commands: Invoke-AzsSupportServiceFabricCommand -Ring <Ring> -ScriptBlock { Get-ServiceFabricClusterHealth }. Handles SF connection automatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportServiceFabricCommand

symptom: Need to verify Service Fabric runtime version consistency across Azure Stack Hub nodes or after upgrade
solution: Use Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricRuntimeVersion [-Ring <Ring>] to check SF runtime version across all cluster nodes. If no ring specified, checks all SF rings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Fabric.ps1/Get-AzsSupportServiceFabricRuntimeVersion

symptom: Azure Stack Hub Network Controller has leaked/orphaned ILB address mapping entries causing IP address conflicts or network resource allocation failures
rootCause: When network resources (internal load balancers) are deleted or modified, the address mapping in Network Controller may not be properly cleaned up, leaving stale entries that can cause IP conflicts
solution: Use Repair-AzsSupportLeakedAddressMapping cmdlet with -SubnetInstanceId (subnet instanceID of the invalid resource) and -IpAddress (leaked IP address) parameters to remove the leaked ILB mapping from Network Controller. Optionally specify -NetworkController if auto-detection is unavailable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Network.Sdn.ps1/Repair-AzsSupportLeakedAddressMapping

symptom: Azure Stack Hub: Group policy machine policy file header integrity check fails, indicating the group policy machine store file is corrupted
rootCause: Group policy machine store file header integrity issue; file corruption in the GP machine policy store on Azure Stack Hub nodes
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIGPMachinePolicyCorruption from Azs.Support Module (CSSTools) to validate the file header integrity. Use -Remediate flag to trigger automated remediation steps
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/ActiveDirectory/GroupPolicy_Corruption/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIGPMachinePolicyCorruption

symptom: Azure Stack Hub: Duplicate infrastructure or tenant virtual machines detected in Hyper-V, causing resource conflicts or operational inconsistencies
rootCause: VM duplication in the Hyper-V layer on Azure Stack Hub compute nodes; duplicate infra or tenant VMs registered in Hyper-V
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKIDuplicateVMs from Azs.Support Module (CSSTools) to identify duplicate infra or tenant VMs in Hyper-V. Review output to determine affected VMs and coordinate remediation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Compute/Duplicate_VMs/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKIDuplicateVMs

symptom: Azure Stack Hub: Infrastructure VMs or tenant VMs fail to start up; VMs stuck in off or failed state due to missing disk ACL permissions
rootCause: Disks ACL missing on infrastructure VMs on Azure Stack Hub compute nodes; incorrect or absent access control list on VM disk files causing startup failures
solution: Run Test-AzSSupportKIDiskACLMissing from Azs.Support Module (CSSTools). Use -AllVMs to test all VMs, -ComputerName for specific VMs (use Hyper-V VM names), or -Remediate for automated ACL remediation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Compute/InfraVMs_Disks_ACL_Missing/script.ps1/Test-AzSSupportKIDiskACLMissing

symptom: Azure Stack Hub: Cluster Shared Volume (CSV) mount point references a GUID instead of a local path, causing potential storage access issues
rootCause: Invalid CSV mount point configuration on Azure Stack Hub; the mount point path is referencing a GUID rather than a valid local path
solution: Run Test-AzsSupportKICSVMountPoint from Azs.Support Module (CSSTools) to validate that CSV mount points reference local paths and not GUIDs. Use -Remediate flag for automated remediation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/knownIssues/Compute/Invalid_CSV_MountPoint/script.ps1/Test-AzsSupportKICSVMountPoint

symptom: Azure Local SFF (Private Preview 1): Provisioning new machine fails at review+create step with error 'Resource name(s) prov-al_sff_<hash> already exists' despite no existing onboarding service resource present
rootCause: Onboarding service resource name only accepts alphanumeric characters and hyphens; underscores in site/cluster names generate invalid resource names that fail validation with a misleading already-exists error (Bug 33075639)
solution: Use only alphanumeric characters and hyphens (no underscores) in Azure Local SFF site names and cluster names to avoid invalid onboarding service resource name generation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Small Form Factor/Readiness/Lab deployments/Deploying Azure Local Small Form Factor with Virtualised Hardware (Private Preview 1 release)

symptom: Azure Local SFF (Private Preview 1): Cluster creation fails at custom location creation phase with DNS-1123 validation error when instance/cluster name contains underscores
rootCause: Kubernetes namespace name derived from the cluster instance name must comply with DNS-1123 label naming convention (alphanumeric and hyphens only); underscores are not valid (Bug 33143274)
solution: Use only alphanumeric characters and hyphens (no underscores) in Azure Local SFF cluster instance names to comply with DNS-1123 naming requirements
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Small Form Factor/Readiness/Lab deployments/Deploying Azure Local Small Form Factor with Virtualised Hardware (Private Preview 1 release)

symptom: Azure Local SFF (Private Preview 1): Cluster creation fails with error 'Device pool internal resource NodeEntityCreate failed'
rootCause: Service principal for Microsoft.AzureStack-CloudManagementApp must be created with specific App ID (f76c49e3-2a2f-4584-ac3b-0a2ccd30cce2) via Azure CLI with tenant-level permissions; SPN was not pre-created in the environment
solution: Create the Microsoft.AzureStack-CloudManagementApp SPN with App ID f76c49e3-2a2f-4584-ac3b-0a2ccd30cce2 via Azure CLI (requires tenant-level permissions). Note: this requirement was removed starting from Private Preview 2 (PrP 2)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Small Form Factor/Readiness/Lab deployments/Deploying Azure Local Small Form Factor with Virtualised Hardware (Private Preview 1 release)

symptom: Azure Local SFF (Private Preview 2): SFF cluster 'Current State' field intermittently changes to 'Failed' after a deployment that was reported as succeeded
rootCause: Known product bug (Bug 34009472) causing intermittent incorrect state reporting in the SFF cluster Current State field after deployment completion
solution: Known bug (Bug 34009472) - monitor the cluster state; if it recovers on its own the underlying operation may still be successful. Raise ICM against Azure Local SFF engineering team if state does not recover
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Small Form Factor/Readiness/Lab deployments/Deploying Azure Local Small Form Factor with Virtualised Hardware (Private Preview 2 release)

symptom: Azure Local SFF (Private Preview 2): Arc-connected cluster shows 'Connecting' status despite some connectivity already being established after cluster creation
rootCause: Kubernetes Arc bug (Task 33380530) - Cluster Metadata Operator behaves unexpectedly during bootup when unable to connect to API Server; same issue observed in Nexus NAKS (Incident-628027194)
solution: Known K8s Arc bug (Task 33380530). Wait for the cluster to fully connect; if status persists, check Cluster Metadata Operator logs. Engage Arc platform team via ICM if needed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Small Form Factor/Readiness/Lab deployments/Deploying Azure Local Small Form Factor with Virtualised Hardware (Private Preview 2 release)

symptom: Azure Local SFF (Private Preview 2): Network connectivity issues on host Windows Server after enabling DHCP; unexpected network disruption or IP assignment conflicts on corporate network adapters
rootCause: When DHCP is enabled on Windows Server host for SFF deployment, it binds to CorpExtSW network adapter by default, causing corporate network conflicts
solution: After enabling DHCP, open the DHCP management app, right-click on your server, select 'Add/Remove Bindings' > IPv4, and untick CorpExtSW to prevent DHCP from binding to the corporate external switch
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Small Form Factor/Readiness/Lab deployments/Deploying Azure Local Small Form Factor with Virtualised Hardware (Private Preview 2 release)

symptom: Azure Stack Hub VHD page blob has incorrect padding after being checked out from ACS (Azure Consistent Storage)
rootCause: Checked-out VHD page blob contains incorrect padding bytes in ACS storage, which can cause data integrity issues
solution: Run Repair-AzsSupportIncorrectPaddingForCheckedOutVhdBlob -AccountName <storageAccount> -ContainerName <container> -BlobName <blobName> [-Credential <domainAdminCred>] [-AcsVmName <acsVm>] to detect and repair the padding issue on the affected ACS VM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Acs.ps1/Repair-AzsSupportIncorrectPaddingForCheckedOutVhdBlob

symptom: Azure Stack Hub ACS storage blob resize (shrink) operation fails or is blocked without explicit flag
rootCause: By default, blob shrinking is not allowed in ACS unless the -Force flag is explicitly specified; enlargement is allowed without restrictions
solution: Use Resize-AzsSupportBlob -AccountName <sa> -ContainerName <ctn> -BlobName <blob> -NewSize <sizeBytes> -Force to shrink a blob. Omit -Force for standard enlargement. Parameters: -AccountName, -ContainerName, -BlobName, -NewSize, -Force (optional), -Credential, -AcsVmName
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Acs.ps1/Resize-AzsSupportBlob

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infrastructure hosts experience system bottleneck or performance degradation after upgrading to version 2108 or later, particularly under high workload
rootCause: Azure Stack Hub 2108 introduced capture of a large number of network and Hyper-V performance counters at 1-second interval, causing bottleneck on high-workload systems. Certain unnecessary network trace capture files also accumulated.
solution: Run Remove-AzsSupportNetworkPerformanceCounters to: (1) remove aggressive network perf counter config files from all infra hosts, (2) adjust host and storage perf config intervals from 1s/10s to 30 seconds, (3) remove unnecessary network trace config files from 2108. Note: trace collector must be in a stoppable state; if trace collector is in bad state (cannot be stopped), the cmdlet will halt and require manual cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Diagnostics.ps1/Remove-AzsSupportNetworkPerformanceCounters

symptom: Azure Stack Hub diagnostic fileshare accumulates old or oversized diagnostic files consuming excessive disk space
rootCause: No automatic cleanup policy for diagnostic fileshare files; files open via SMB and reserved/special folders with different retention config are excluded from deletion
solution: Run Start-AzsSupportStoragePruneDiagnosticFileShare [-DeleteFilesOlderThanDays <days>] [-DeleteFilesBiggerThanGB <gb>] to prune diagnostic files. Files open via SMB are safely skipped. Reserved/special folders excluded. Example: Start-AzsSupportStoragePruneDiagnosticFileShare -DeleteFilesOlderThanDays 30 -DeleteFilesBiggerThanGB 5
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Diagnostics.ps1/Start-AzsSupportStoragePruneDiagnosticFileShare

symptom: Azure Stack Hub infra VM or infrastructure host is running low on disk space
rootCause: Accumulated files on infra VMs/hosts not cleaned according to recycle settings in appSettings.json
solution: Run Clear-AzSSupportDiskSpace [-ComputerName <name1>,<name2>] to clear disk space based on recycle settings in appSettings.json. Tracks cleanup records in AzS.Support log. Example: Clear-AzSSupportDiskSpace -ComputerName "PREFIX-Node01","PREFIX-XRP01"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.ps1/Clear-AzSSupportDiskSpace

symptom: Azure Stack Hub blob has an infinite or stuck lease preventing blob/container deletion (CannotDeleteBlob or similar errors)
solution: Use CSSTools Invoke-AzsSupportBreakBlobLease to forcibly break the blob lease: `Invoke-AzsSupportBreakBlobLease -AccountName <sa> -ContainerName <ctn> -BlobName <blob> [-LeaseBreakingPeriod <period>]`. Requires -Credential (domain admin) and -AcsVmName if not running on ACS VM directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Acs.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportBreakBlobLease

symptom: Azure Stack Hub blob is stuck in checked-out/locked state with an orphaned acquisition ID, preventing access or modification
solution: Use CSSTools Invoke-AzsSupportCheckInBlob: `Invoke-AzsSupportCheckInBlob -AccountName <sa> -ContainerName <ctn> -BlobName <blob> -AcquisitionId <id>`. Use -Force flag to release without requiring the acquisition ID when ID is unknown.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Acs.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportCheckInBlob

symptom: Azure Stack Hub tenant VM disk VHD file is fragmented on s-cluster causing I/O performance degradation
solution: Use CSSTools Invoke-AzsSupportDiskVhdDefrag: `Invoke-AzsSupportDiskVhdDefrag -VhdPath \\su1fileserver.xxx\path\to\disk.vhd`. Provide the full UNC share path to the VHD on s-cluster storage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Acs.ps1/Invoke-AzsSupportDiskVhdDefrag

symptom: Azure Stack Hub empty blob container needs to be rebalanced or migrated from one s-cluster share to another (storage rebalancing or pre-maintenance relocation)
solution: Use CSSTools Move-AzsSupportEmptyBlobContainer: `Move-AzsSupportEmptyBlobContainer -AccountName <sa> -ContainerName <ctn> -DestShare <share>`. Container must be empty before moving. Requires -Credential (domain admin) and -AcsVmName for remote execution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Azs.Support Module/modules/AzureStack.Hub.Storage.Acs.ps1/Move-AzsSupportEmptyBlobContainer

symptom: AT&T customer raises a Nexus billing query (direct support case or via ASMS collaboration case)
rootCause: AT&T has a bespoke contract with specific billing terms; standard Azure billing mechanics and meters are not used; Nexus bills are generated manually based on telemetry from AT&T systems rather than standard Azure meters
solution: Reach out to Jason Ryberg (jason.ryberg@microsoft.com) with case details and agree next steps to respond to AT&T. The AT&T contact raising the SR is typically unaware of contractual details; advise them to contact the AT&T account person responsible for the Nexus contract
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Tools and Processes/Case Handling/AT&T Nexus Billing issues

symptom: Customer submits a Nexus support case requesting a new feature or reporting unsupported Nexus functionality
solution: Follow guidance at https://dev.azure.com/msazuredev/AzureForOperatorsIndustry/_wiki/wikis/AzureForOperatorsIndustry.wiki/30560 (When Support Requests are design questions or feature requests). Check existing tracked feature requests at https://dev.azure.com/msazuredev/AzureForOperatorsIndustry/_queries/query/e3c5246c-3084-4443-9a4c-434975b4138b/ (AT&T-requested features have ART-<NNN> prefix on their title)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Local Nexus/Tools and Processes/Case Handling/New feature requests and unsupported functionality cases

symptom: VM deployment fails when using custom user image created in Azure Stack Hub version 1907
rootCause: Bug in Azure Stack Hub 1907 image handling; orchestration issue between IBHU and non-HA VM repair during image-based deployments
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.12.44 (KB 4517473, released Aug 16 2019). Fixes orchestration between IBHU and non-HA VM repair and updates URP to use ScopedRepair action plan.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.12.44

symptom: Internal secret rotation fails on Azure Stack Hub environments originally deployed on version 1901 or earlier
rootCause: Bug in internal secret rotation process introduced in 1907 that does not account for older deployment configurations from 1901 and earlier
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.15.50 (KB 4520315, released Sep 5 2019). Suppresses NC cluster certificate warning in test-azurestack to unblock PNU and internal secret rotation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.15.50

symptom: Azure Stack Hub operators cannot perform update or secret rotation because test-azurestack updatereadiness blocks admin operations when a pending secret/certificate expiration is detected
rootCause: test-azurestack updatereadiness incorrectly surfaced NC cluster certificate warnings as blocking conditions, preventing PNU and secret rotation operations
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.15.50 (KB 4520315). NC cluster certificate warning suppressed in test-azurestack updatereadiness so it no longer blocks admin operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.15.50

symptom: OEM Firmware updates required for Azure Stack Hub 1908 upgrade fail to install on 1907; false BMC saturation alerts generate high alert volume in 1907
rootCause: OEM firmware update installation had reliability issues (FRU timeout too short for Scale-Outs exceeding 6 hours); BMC runner generating false positive saturation alerts
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.17.54 (KB 4523826, released Sep 27 2019). Increases FRU timeout for Scale-Outs, fixes OEM update preparation reliability, and disables false-positive BMC saturation alerts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.17.54

symptom: Deleting a load balancer in Azure Stack Hub 1907 results in orphaned resources; LB object persists in Network Controller (NC) after deletion
rootCause: Load balancer deletion did not propagate to delete the corresponding object in Network Controller (NC), leaving orphaned NC resources
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.17.54 (KB 4523826). Fix ensures deleting an LB also deletes the object in NC, preventing orphaned resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.17.54

symptom: DiskRP does not update application manifest parameters after completing Internal Secret Rotation in Azure Stack Hub 1907; Service Fabric ImageStoreService replicas are down
rootCause: DiskRP service did not refresh application manifest parameters following Internal Secret Rotation; unused SF DNS service could crash XRP during future updates; SF ImageStoreService replica failures
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.18.56 (KB 4528552, released Oct 29 2019). Fixes DiskRP manifest parameter update after secret rotation; disables unused SF DNS service; backports repair of SF ImageStoreService replicas.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.18.56

symptom: Non-HA VMs (NonHAVMs) fail to start after an Azure Stack Hub update in version 1907; VMs remain stopped post-update
rootCause: NonHAVMs initialization issue with guardian certificates; RestoreNonHAVMs process did not handle guardian cert initialization correctly during Azure Stack startup after update
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.26.70 (KB 4541348, released Feb 25 2020). Fixes NonHAVMs guardian certificate initialization; fixes RestoreNonHAVMs initialization issue during Azure Stack update.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.26.70

symptom: External certificate expiration alerts continue appearing on Azure Stack Hub admin portal even after successfully completing external secret rotation
rootCause: Certificate alerting was not scoped to only certificates bound to external endpoints; alerts fired for internal certificates that do not require external rotation
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.29.80 (KB 4555650, released Apr 2 2020). Certificate alerting fixed to only alert on certificates bound to external endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.29.80

symptom: Azure Stack Hub marketplace downloads fail with a certificate validation error
rootCause: Azure Bridge certificates were missing on WAS (Windows Azure Stack) and XRP VMs, causing certificate validation failure during marketplace download operations
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.30.83 (KB 4562962, released Nov 6 2020). Installs Azure Bridge Certificates on WAS and XRP VMs to resolve marketplace certificate validation errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.30.83

symptom: Test-AzureStack reports false Fabric Ring health failures (misdiagnosis) in Azure Stack Hub 1907; OEM firmware update fails due to alternate signer check blocking valid update scripts
rootCause: Test-AzureStack incorrectly diagnosed Fabric Ring health status; alternate signer checking was too restrictive for OEM firmware update scripts
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.7.35 (KB 4515310, released Jul 25 2019). Corrects Test-AzureStack Fabric Ring health diagnosis; skips alternate signer checking for OEM firmware update scripts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.7.35

symptom: Azure Stack Hub environments originally deployed before version 1809 are missing OEM-related categories in CloudParameters, causing OEM update compatibility failures in 1907
rootCause: Pre-1809 deployments were not provisioned with certain OEM-related CloudParameters categories required for OEM compatibility in later versions
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.8.37 (KB 4515650, released Aug 2 2019). Adds missing OEM parameter categories to pre-1809 deployments via BeforeAzureStack inline patch.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.8.37

symptom: Azure Stack Hub admin portal does not display download progress for update packages; download progress bar missing or stalled
rootCause: URP (Update Resource Provider) was not reporting download progress to the admin portal UI; missing telemetry/UI update path for download progress in 1908
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1908.11.47 (KB 4535000, released Dec 18 2019). Improves admin portal to show download progress of Azure Stack Hub update packages.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1908/1.1908.11.47

symptom: Setting VM LicenseType to value None in Azure Stack Hub 1908 fails; None is not recognized as a valid LicenseType value
rootCause: CRP (Compute Resource Provider) did not handle LicenseType value None as valid; memory cache issue with virtual identifier lookup by network identity token
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1908.11.47 (KB 4535000). CRP updated to accept LicenseType None; memory cache for virtual identifier lookup by network identity token fixed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1908/1.1908.11.47

symptom: Azure Stack Hub 1907 update preparation steps are unreliable or stall; update preparation UX shows incorrect status
rootCause: WaitAndReceiveJob was too permissive of PowerShell states; UX reporting gaps in preparation phase; multiple preparation step reliability issues
solution: Apply hotfix 1.1907.18.56 (KB 4528552). Multiple preparation UX improvements and reliability fixes including stricter PowerShell state handling in WaitAndReceiveJob.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAdaptiveCloud/AzureAdaptiveCloud.wiki:/Azure Stack Hub/Builds/1907/1.1907.18.56
